summary: Reverse-engineer the integration sync engine subsystems + lifecycle GQL ops from current source to ground live-test assertions

result.mechanics:
The watermark subsystem drives per-entity-map, per-direction incremental sync. State lives in one row per (EntityMapID, Direction) in table CompanyIntegrationSyncWatermark (entity "MJ: Company Integration Sync Watermarks", base view vwCompanyIntegrationSyncWatermarks). Columns (entity_subclasses.ts:52534-52623): EntityMapID (FK), Direction (nvarchar(50), List 'Pull'|'Push', default 'Pull'), WatermarkType (nvarchar(50), List 'Timestamp'|'Cursor'|'ChangeToken'|'Version', default 'Timestamp'), WatermarkValue (nvarchar(MAX), nullable), LastSyncAt (datetimeoffset, nullable), RecordsSynced (int, default 0).

READ AT START: ProcessPullSync calls watermarkService.Load(entityMapID, user, 'Pull') (IntegrationEngine.ts:1048). Load (WatermarkService.ts:18-34) RunViews "MJ: Company Integration Sync Watermarks" filtered by EntityMapID and Direction, OrderBy 'LastSyncAt DESC', MaxRows 1 — newest row wins. The chosen value becomes initialWatermark, but null when config.fullSync (IntegrationEngine.ts:1065). Then A6 validation: ValidateWatermark(value, type) (WatermarkService.ts:89-105) — Timestamp requires !isNaN(Date.parse), Version requires /^\d+$/, Cursor/ChangeToken just non-empty. Invalid → console.warn "Invalid watermark '...' resetting to full fetch" and initialWatermark=null (IntegrationEngine.ts:1066-1073).

SOURCE-SIDE FILTER: the engine does NOT build the filter; it passes WatermarkValue into FetchContext.WatermarkValue (BaseIntegrationConnector.ts:109; set IntegrationEngine.ts:1177) and the connector builds the incremental query. HubSpot CRM: when WatermarkValue present → FetchChangesViaSearch (HubSpotConnector.ts:2438-2439) posts CRM /search with filterGroups [{ propertyName: dateField, operator:'GTE', value:String(watermarkMs) }], sorts (dateField ASC, hs_object_id ASC); dateField = 'lastmodifieddate' for contacts else 'hs_lastmodifieddate' (GetWatermarkField, :3100). Non-CRM: serverIncrementalParam appended to URL, else client-side filter on updatedAt/updated/createdAt/hs_lastmodifieddate > watermarkMs (HubSpotConnector.ts:2681-2728). The generic BaseRESTIntegrationConnector.FetchFlat does NOT itself apply a watermark filter.

ADVANCE: each batch's FetchBatchResult.NewWatermarkValue (BaseIntegrationConnector.ts:159), when present, sets currentWatermark (IntegrationEngine.ts:1333-1334). HubSpot computes NewWatermarkValue only on the final page (!hasMore) via FindLatestDate over non-deleted records (HubSpotConnector.ts:2916-2920). Mid-sync, when batch.HasMore, UpdateProgress(entityMapID, totalWritten) bumps RecordsSynced + LastSyncAt without touching WatermarkValue (IntegrationEngine.ts:1321-1323; WatermarkService.ts:69-80). Final persistence on a CLEAN fetch (IntegrationEngine.ts:1385-1414): finalWatermark = if currentWatermark exists → (config.fullSync ? new Date().toISOString() : incrementalWatermark) else new Date().toISOString(); then watermarkService.Update(entityMapID, finalWatermark, user, 'Pull') → result.WatermarkAfter.

SAFE-FLOOR HOLD-BACK: lastCleanWatermark tracks the high-water of the last fully-clean batch. After advancing currentWatermark, the floor is raised ONLY when result.RecordsErrored === erroredBeforeApply (zero new errors this batch) (IntegrationEngine.ts:1335-1337). At final save, on an incremental with result.RecordsErrored > 0 the engine holds the watermark to lastCleanWatermark ?? currentWatermark (NOT the latest), so the failed window is re-fetched next run and reconciled by idempotent upsert + content-hash skip (IntegrationEngine.ts:1405-1409). A full sync always advances to "now" regardless of errors.

WATERMARK FALLBACK SAVE: on a clean fetch where the connector returned no NewWatermarkValue (empty result set, or a source object with no modstamp column), the else-branch still writes finalWatermark = new Date().toISOString() (IntegrationEngine.ts:1410-1413). This guarantees a watermark row exists for bookkeeping so the next incremental does not re-issue an unfiltered full-scan query. CreateNewWatermark defaults WatermarkType='Timestamp' (WatermarkService.ts:216-223).

ORTHOGONAL MODES sharing the same row's WatermarkType/WatermarkValue: (1) Keyset/seek — when connector.StableOrderingKey(objectName) != null (IntegrationEngine.ts:1096), initialWatermark forced null; an interrupted scan persists the last AfterKey via SaveKeysetPosition → WatermarkType='Cursor', WatermarkValue=afterKey (WatermarkService.ts:116-133); a clean scan calls ClearKeysetPosition → WatermarkType='Cursor', WatermarkValue=null (WatermarkService.ts:141-151). Resume reads watermark.WatermarkType==='Cursor' && WatermarkValue (IntegrationEngine.ts:1115-1116) and emits log event 'sync.resume.keyset'. Durable checkpoint every 25 batches also persists the keyset position (IntegrationEngine.ts:1347-1363). (2) Partition (Merkle) reconcile — opt-in via entityMap.Configuration JSON {partitionReconcile:true, partitionCount} (IntegrationEngine.ts:2090-2105); forces initialWatermark null; rollup map persisted via SavePartitionRollups → WatermarkType='ChangeToken', WatermarkValue=JSON (WatermarkService.ts:159-175); LoadPartitionRollups only reads when WatermarkType==='ChangeToken' (WatermarkService.ts:181-195). In both keyset and partition modes result.WatermarkAfter is null (IntegrationEngine.ts:1377,1384).

PUSH direction: a parallel 'Push' watermark stores the last Record-Changes ChangedAt timestamp; Load/Update with direction='Push' (IntegrationEngine.ts:1456,1539).

testableAssertions.what: After a clean Pull sync, exactly one Pull watermark row exists per entity map with a non-null Timestamp value and updated LastSyncAt
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Query vwCompanyIntegrationSyncWatermarks WHERE EntityMapID='<id>' AND Direction='Pull'; assert 1 row, WatermarkType='Timestamp', WatermarkValue parseable as ISO date, LastSyncAt recent
testableAssertions.signals: columns Direction='Pull', WatermarkType='Timestamp', WatermarkValue, LastSyncAt, RecordsSynced; SyncResult.WatermarkAfter non-null

testableAssertions.what: Watermark fallback save: a clean sync of an object whose connector returns no NewWatermarkValue (empty/no-modstamp) still writes a watermark row so next run is not a full-scan
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Sync an entity map with zero source records (or modstamp-less object); assert a Pull watermark row now exists with WatermarkType='Timestamp' and WatermarkValue ~= now() even though no records were written

testableAssertions.signals: CompanyIntegrationRunDetail counts; FetchContext.WatermarkValue passed to connector; HubSpot search filter operator GTE on lastmodifieddate/hs_lastmodifieddate

testableAssertions.what: Keyset connector: interrupted scan persists Cursor resume key; clean scan clears it; next run resumes from the key
testableAssertions.howToAssert: For a StableOrderingKey object, cancel mid-scan then re-run; assert watermark row WatermarkType='Cursor' with the last AfterKey, and log 'sync.resume.keyset'
testableAssertions.signals: WatermarkType='Cursor', WatermarkValue=afterKey (then null after clean scan); logger event 'sync.resume.keyset' with resumeAfterKey; FetchBatchResult.NextAfterKeyValue / FetchContext.AfterKeyValue

testableAssertions.what: Partition (Merkle) reconcile stores rollup snapshot as ChangeToken on the same watermark row and skips unchanged partitions
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Enable via entity map Configuration {"partitionReconcile":true}; run twice unchanged; assert watermark WatermarkType='ChangeToken' with JSON value and second run RecordsSkipped≈all
testableAssertions.signals: WatermarkType='ChangeToken', WatermarkValue=JSON map; entityMap.Configuration JSON keys partitionReconcile/partitionCount; SyncResult.WatermarkAfter null; RecordsSkipped

testableAssertions.what: Mid-sync progress updates RecordsSynced and LastSyncAt without changing WatermarkValue between batches
testableAssertions.howToAssert: During a multi-batch sync, poll the watermark row; assert RecordsSynced increases and LastSyncAt refreshes while WatermarkValue stays at the prior value until final save
testableAssertions.signals: UpdateProgress only fires when batch.HasMore (IntegrationEngine.ts:1321); columns RecordsSynced, LastSyncAt unchanged WatermarkValue

testableAssertions.what: Push direction maintains its own watermark row separate from Pull
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Run a Push/Bidirectional sync; query watermarks WHERE Direction='Push'; assert a distinct row exists keyed by last Record-Changes ChangedAt
testableAssertions.signals: Direction='Push' column; Load/Update direction='Push' (IntegrationEngine.ts:1456,1539); OrderBy LastSyncAt DESC selects newest

knownGapsOrBugs: Keyset and timestamp watermark are treated as MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE: declaring StableOrderingKey for an object forces initialWatermark=null (IntegrationEngine.ts:1086-1096), so a connector that declares StableOrderingKey for an object that DOES have a server-side date incremental silently turns every incremental into a full scan. Documented as an intentional known limitation; the orthogonal-signals fix is future work.

WatermarkAfter is only surfaced in the in-process SyncResult (types.ts:139); it is NOT exposed as a distinct GraphQL field on the sync result type in IntegrationDiscoveryResolver — a live test must read the persisted watermark row via the entity/view, not from the mutation payload.

Load selects newest by OrderBy 'LastSyncAt DESC' MaxRows 1 (WatermarkService.ts:27-28). If duplicate rows exist for the same (EntityMapID, Direction) and LastSyncAt ties, the winner is non-deterministic; there is no unique constraint enforced in WatermarkService — duplicate-row creation is possible if Load races (concurrent first-sync of the same map).

Safe-floor hold-back only re-anchors the timestamp watermark; for connectors whose NewWatermarkValue is computed only on the final page (e.g. HubSpot, HubSpotConnector.ts:2916-2920), an incremental that errors before reaching the last page leaves currentWatermark unchanged from initial, so lastCleanWatermark also stays at initial — correct (no advance) but means partial progress within an errored run is not preserved as a floor.

HubSpot non-CRM client-side watermark filtering checks a fixed set of date keys ['updatedAt','updated','createdAt','hs_lastmodifieddate'] (HubSpotConnector.ts:2720-2724); an object whose modified-date property is none of these will fail to filter incrementally and re-emit all records every run (still idempotent via upsert/content-hash, but no true incremental).

ValidateWatermark for ChangeToken/Cursor only checks length>0 (WatermarkService.ts:99-101), so a corrupt-but-non-empty Cursor/ChangeToken value passes validation; corruption is only caught downstream (LoadPartitionRollups try/catch at WatermarkService.ts:187-192 falls back to full reconcile; a corrupt Cursor resume key would be passed to the connector as AfterKey).

result.mechanics:
CONTENT-HASH change detection lets the engine skip the per-record DB load AND write for unchanged rows when a source has no usable watermark (e.g. YourMembership re-fetches every record every time).

ALGORITHM (packages/Integration/engine/src/ContentHash.ts:30-52): `computeContentHash(fields)` canonicalizes the record by RECURSIVELY SORTING object keys (so fetch-order variation never produces a spurious "changed"), keeping ARRAY order (semantically meaningful), DROPPING keys whose value is `undefined` (absent == explicit-undefined), serializing Dates by `.toISOString()`, and `null` is canonicalized to the literal string `'null'` (so `null` is DISTINCT from absent). It then `createHash('sha256').update(canonical).digest('hex')` → a 64-char lowercase hex string. CRITICAL: it hashes the MAPPED field values (`record.MappedFields`, what lands in MJ), NOT the raw external payload — a change in an unmapped source field does not (correctly) count as a change.

WHERE WRITTEN: `SetStandardIntegrationFields` (IntegrationEngine.ts:2764-2794) sets `entity.Set('__mj_integration_ContentHash', computeContentHash(record.MappedFields ?? {}))` on EVERY create and update, but ONLY if `entity.Fields` contains that column (hasField guard) — a no-op on tables predating the feature. The column name constant is `CONTENT_HASH_COLUMN = '__mj_integration_ContentHash'` (ContentHash.ts:18). DDL: NVARCHAR(64) NULL on SQL Server, VARCHAR(64) NULL on Postgres, added in CREATE TABLE via DDLGenerator.StandardColumns (DDLGenerator.ts:271,285) for new tables and ALTER-added to existing tables via SchemaEvolution.EnsureStandardColumns (SchemaEvolution.ts:121). Its column description (DDLGenerator.ts:126) is "SHA-256 (hex) of the last-synced external field values...".

SKIP-UNCHANGED FAST PATH (two layers, both in the UPDATE path):
1. Content-hash precheck (IntegrationEngine.ts:2405-2416): `PrefetchContentHashes(batch)` (lines 2478-2518) does ONE RunView per batch — `Fields: [pk, '__mj_integration_ContentHash']`, `ExtraFilter: '<pk> IN (...)'`, `ResultType: 'simple'` — building `Map<MatchedMJRecordID, storedHash>`. In `UpdateRecord`, if `stored && stored === computeContentHash(record.MappedFields ?? {})` → `result.RecordsSkipped++` and `return` IMMEDIATELY, before `GetEntityObject`/`InnerLoad` — so NO DB load and NO write. The record-map row and the target row's `__mj_UpdatedAt` are left untouched (no Save() is called on this path).
2. Dirty-flag fallback (IntegrationEngine.ts:2433-2440): for entities WITHOUT the hash column, after `SetEntityFields`+`SetStandardIntegrationFields`, if `!entity.Dirty` → `result.RecordsSkipped++` and `return` (no Save). Uses MJ's built-in dirty tracking.

PrefetchContentHashes returns `undefined` (disabling the fast path, dirty-flag path runs) when: no Update record with a MatchedMJRecordID in the batch; entity has no ContentHash field; entity has a COMPOSITE PK (`pkFields.length !== 1` — single-PK-only fast path); or the read throws (best-effort try/catch).

PARTITION-ROLLUP (Merkle) MODE (HashDiff.ts + IntegrationEngine.applyViaPartitionReconcile:2122-2196): an opt-in coarser layer for watermark-less full scans. Records bucket by STABLE IDENTITY (`partitionKeyForIdentity(ExternalID, partitionCount=256)` = first 4 bytes of SHA-256(identity) mod partitionCount); each bucket folds its per-record `computeContentHash` values via `partitionRollupHash` (per-record hashes SORTED → order-independent, length-prefixed, SHA-256'd); `diffPartitions(newRollups, storedRollups)` yields changed/added/removed; only changed+added partitions are deep-applied (match+upsert), unchanged partitions add `recs.length` to BOTH RecordsProcessed and RecordsSkipped and are skipped entirely. On `config.fullSync` the stored snapshot is treated as EMPTY → every partition re-applies. Snapshot persisted to the Pull watermark via `watermarkService.SavePartitionRollups`. Emits `sync.partition.reconcile`.

WATERMARK INTERACTION (IntegrationEngine.ts:1375-1413): content-hash is the change-detector for sources with NO watermark; it doesn't replace the watermark but makes re-scans cheap. In partition-reconcile mode `result.WatermarkAfter = null` (the watermark row holds the rollup snapshot, not a timestamp, 1377). For keyset connectors a clean scan CLEARS the keyset position and sets `WatermarkAfter = null` (1383-1384) — relying on content-hash to keep unchanged rows write-free on the next full re-seek. On an incremental with errored records the watermark holds to the last clean batch's value so the failed window re-fetches; "the idempotent upsert + content-hash skip reconcile it" (1405-1408).

RESULT/PERSISTENCE: skipped records increment `result.RecordsSkipped` (types.ts:135,162). FinalizeRun sets `run.TotalRecords = result.RecordsProcessed` (skipped rows ARE counted in Processed) and `run.Status = result.RecordsErrored > 0 ? 'Failed' : 'Success'` — skips do not fail a run. Run invariant: Processed == Created + Updated + Skipped + Errored. The `sync.entity-map.complete` log event carries `recordsSkipped` → mirrored to the structured artifact as `skipped` (SyncLogger.ts:205). GraphQL exposes it as `IntegrationRunCountsOutput.Skipped` (resolver toCountsOutput:4139-4147) and `SyncProgressOutput.RecordsProcessed` (resolver:717,3849).

SEPARATE REUSE: BaseRESTIntegrationConnector.ToExternalRecord:995 uses `computeContentHash(raw)` (RAW payload, not mapped) as a synthetic ExternalID when a PK is missing/partial — distinct purpose (stable identity), same hash function.

testableAssertions.what: An unchanged record on a re-sync is skipped (not re-written) — RecordsSkipped increments and the target row's __mj_UpdatedAt does NOT advance
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Run a full sync twice with identical source data on an entity that has __mj_integration_ContentHash. After run 1 note the target row's __mj_UpdatedAt and __mj_integration_LastSyncedAt. After run 2, query: target row __mj_UpdatedAt is unchanged (no Save on skip path), and the run's Skipped count > 0. GQL: read IntegrationRunCountsOutput.Skipped or SyncProgressOutput.RecordsProcessed.
testableAssertions.signals: __mj_integration_ContentHash column (NVARCHAR(64)); RecordsSkipped / counts.Skipped / recordsSkipped; __mj_UpdatedAt unchanged; sync.entity-map.complete event field recordsSkipped

testableAssertions.what: The stored ContentHash equals the SHA-256 hex of the canonicalized MAPPED fields and is exactly 64 lowercase hex chars
testableAssertions.howToAssert: After a sync, SELECT __mj_integration_ContentHash FROM the target table; assert it matches /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/. Independently compute computeContentHash(mappedFields) for that record and assert equality. Reorder source JSON keys and re-sync → hash (and skip behavior) is unchanged.
testableAssertions.signals: Column __mj_integration_ContentHash; value matches ^[0-9a-f]{64}$; key-order-independent; null distinct from absent; array order preserved

testableAssertions.what: Changing a MAPPED field value flips the hash and forces an UPDATE (RecordsUpdated++, not Skipped); changing only an UNMAPPED source field is skipped
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Sync, then mutate one mapped field upstream and re-sync → the run shows RecordsUpdated incremented and the stored __mj_integration_ContentHash changed. Mutate only a non-mapped source field and re-sync → RecordsSkipped++ and hash unchanged.
testableAssertions.signals: RecordsUpdated vs RecordsSkipped; __mj_integration_ContentHash before/after; sync.record.saved outcome 'updated' vs 'skipped'

testableAssertions.what: Composite-PK entities bypass the content-hash precheck and fall through to the dirty-flag skip (PrefetchContentHashes returns undefined for pkFields.length !== 1)
testableAssertions.howToAssert: On a composite-PK integration entity, re-sync identical data; records are still skipped (RecordsSkipped++) via the !entity.Dirty path, but PrefetchContentHashes performed no batch hash read (the entity was loaded one-by-one). Verify skips still occur and no spurious updates.
testableAssertions.signals: RecordsSkipped still increments; single-PK fast path only (pkFields.length !== 1 → undefined); !entity.Dirty fallback

testableAssertions.what: Partition-rollup (Merkle) mode skips whole unchanged partitions, counting their records as both Processed and Skipped, and emits sync.partition.reconcile
testableAssertions.howToAssert: Enable partition reconcile, sync twice with identical data. Assert sync.partition.reconcile event with skippedPartitions>0, skippedRecords>0, changedPartitions/addedPartitions=0; RecordsProcessed == RecordsSkipped for the unchanged set. Confirm WatermarkAfter is null (rollup snapshot stored, not a timestamp).
testableAssertions.signals: event sync.partition.reconcile fields: totalPartitions, changedPartitions, addedPartitions, removedPartitions, skippedPartitions, skippedRecords, appliedRecords; WatermarkAfter null; watermarkService SavePartitionRollups/LoadPartitionRollups

testableAssertions.what: A forced full sync re-applies every partition / every record despite matching hashes (fullSync treats stored snapshot as empty)
testableAssertions.howToAssert: With partition reconcile + fullSync=true, re-sync identical data → diff treats stored as empty Map, toApply contains all partitions, skippedPartitions=0; run advances watermark to now (new Date().toISOString()).
testableAssertions.signals: config.fullSync → new Map() snapshot; skippedPartitions=0; finalWatermark = now on fullSync

testableAssertions.what: Run does not fail on skips; TotalRecords includes skipped rows and Status is Success when RecordsErrored==0
testableAssertions.howToAssert: After an all-skip re-sync, read CompanyIntegrationRun: Status='Success', TotalRecords == RecordsProcessed (which includes the skipped count). GQL run-counts Skipped reflects them.
testableAssertions.signals: CompanyIntegrationRun.Status='Success'; run.TotalRecords = RecordsProcessed; invariant Processed = Created+Updated+Skipped+Errored

knownGapsOrBugs: The two skip layers count differently in terms of cost but NOT in terms of result: the content-hash precheck (line 2410) skips WITHOUT loading the entity, while the dirty-flag path (line 2437) loads + sets fields first then skips. Both yield RecordsSkipped++; a live test cannot distinguish them from the count alone — it must observe whether a DB read/__mj_UpdatedAt-write occurred (the precheck path leaves zero footprint, the dirty path loads but doesn't Save).

Hash is computed over record.MappedFields, which does NOT include the __mj_integration_* standard columns (those are set AFTER, in SetStandardIntegrationFields). So __mj_integration_LastSyncedAt / ExternalVersion / LastReconciledAt changing does NOT affect the hash — correct by design, but means a row that is 'skipped' deliberately leaves LastSyncedAt/LastReconciledAt STALE (not refreshed) for unchanged records. Documented as a follow-up at line 2811-2813 (LastReconciledAt only updated on actual create/update, so unchanged-but-still-present records appear unreconciled).

computeContentHash falls back to JSON.stringify for non-plain objects other than Date/Array; an unmapped exotic value type (e.g. a class instance) could hash unstably across runs, but MappedFields are normalized primitives so this is largely theoretical.

BaseRESTIntegrationConnector.ts:995 uses computeContentHash(raw) on the RAW payload for synthetic PK-less identity, whereas the change-detection path uses MappedFields. If a PK-less record's raw payload reorders/changes an unmapped field, its synthetic ExternalID (identity) shifts, which can cause it to look like a new record (added) rather than a content change — a subtle divergence between the two hash usages.

PrefetchContentHashes uses string interpolation of IDs into ExtraFilter with single-quote escaping only (`replace(/'/g,"''")`, line 2497); fine for UUID/numeric PKs but is a manual escape rather than parameterization.

Partition skip path (line 2165-2166) adds to BOTH RecordsProcessed and RecordsSkipped to preserve the run invariant; these records never emit per-record sync.record.saved events, so a live test asserting per-record 'skipped' events will NOT see them for partition-skipped records — only the aggregate sync.partition.reconcile event reflects them.

result.subsystem: merkle-partition (opt-in partition / Merkle hash-diff reconcile for watermark-less objects)
result.mechanics:
OPT-IN GATE. Per-entity-map. The path is enabled when the entity map's `Configuration` JSON column contains `{"partitionReconcile": true}` (optionally `"partitionCount": N`). `IntegrationEngine.parseEntityMapConfig` JSON.parses `ICompanyIntegrationEntityMap.Configuration`; `isPartitionReconcileEnabled` returns true only on strict `=== true` (IntegrationEngine.ts:2090-2099). The mode also requires the connector to NOT be a keyset connector: `partitionReconcile = !isKeysetConnector && this.isPartitionReconcileEnabled(entityMap)` where `isKeysetConnector = config.connector.StableOrderingKey(objectName) != null` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1096,1106). Partition count default = 256; `partitionReconcileCount` floors any finite `partitionCount >= 1`, else 256 (IntegrationEngine.ts:2101-2105). When enabled, `initialWatermark = null` because the watermark row holds the rollup snapshot, not a timestamp (IntegrationEngine.ts:1108-1110).

FETCH-THEN-DEFER. In partition mode the per-batch loop does NOT match/apply. Instead `accumulatedMapped.push(...mapped)` buffers the entire fetched set in RAM, `resolved = []`, and `ApplyRecords` is skipped per-batch (IntegrationEngine.ts:1157,1271-1281). Progress logging and `UpdateProgress` are also gated off in this mode (IntegrationEngine.ts:1310). Every fetched `ExternalID` is still added to `fetchedExternalIDs` for the orphan sweep (IntegrationEngine.ts:1264-1266).

POST-LOOP RECONCILE. After the fetch loop, IF `partitionReconcile && fetchCompletedCleanly`, `applyViaPartitionReconcile` runs (IntegrationEngine.ts:1371-1373). A partial fetch (cancel / fetch error / SchemaNotGeneratedError mid-batch / 5000-batch safety limit flips `fetchCompletedCleanly=false`) SKIPS the reconcile entirely — no snapshot is touched, so nothing is mis-skipped (IntegrationEngine.ts:1154,1302,1371).

applyViaPartitionReconcile (IntegrationEngine.ts:2122-2196): (1) identity = `r.ExternalRecord.ExternalID`; partition = `partitionKeyForIdentity(externalID, partitionCount)`. (2) `partitionRecords` buckets the full set by partition string. (3) For each bucket, `partitionRollupHash(recs, r => r.MappedFields)` folds the bucket into one rollup → `newRollups: Map<partition,hash>`. (4) `stored` = empty Map when `config.fullSync` is true (DRIFT REPAIR — forces every partition to be 'added' so all re-apply), else `watermarkService.LoadPartitionRollups(entityMapID)`. (5) `diff = diffPartitions(newRollups, stored)`; `toApply = new Set([...diff.changed, ...diff.added])`. (6) Iterate buckets: partitions NOT in toApply are skipped — `skippedPartitions++`, `skippedRecords += recs.length`, and crucially `result.RecordsProcessed += recs.length; result.RecordsSkipped += recs.length` (to preserve the invariant processed == created+updated+skipped+errored). Partitions in toApply: `matchEngine.Resolve` then `ApplyRecords`, `appliedRecords += recs.length`. (7) On `SchemaNotGeneratedError` it pushes a `CONFIGURATION_ERROR`/Critical error and RETURNS WITHOUT saving rollups (next sync re-reconciles). (8) Only after a clean apply: `watermarkService.SavePartitionRollups(entityMapID, newRollups)`. (9) Emits `sync.partition.reconcile`.

HASH PRIMITIVES (HashDiff.ts, pure/deterministic): `partitionKeyForIdentity(identity, count=256)` = SHA-256(identity), first 8 hex chars → int → `% max(1,count)`, returned as a STRING; keyed by IDENTITY (not content) so a content edit stays in the same bucket and surfaces as a CHANGED partition, never a bucket hop (HashDiff.ts:33-36). `partitionRollupHash(recs, fieldsOf)` = map each record to `computeContentHash(MappedFields)`, SORT the per-record hashes (ORDER-INDEPENDENT — reorder never flags a change), length-prefix `${len}:${hash}`, concat, SHA-256 → 64-char hex; empty partition rolls up to SHA-256 of empty string = `e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855` (HashDiff.ts:86-96). `diffPartitions(local, remote)` → `{changed,added,removed}` sorted: changed = key in BOTH, hashes differ; added = local-only; removed = remote-only (HashDiff.ts:107-130). NOTE: in the call, local=newRollups, remote=stored, so 'added' = partitions new this sync, 'removed' = partitions gone since snapshot. `removed` partitions are NOT deep-applied (they have no records to apply); deletions are handled separately by the orphan sweep.

CHANGETOKEN SNAPSHOT STORE (WatermarkService.ts): The snapshot is persisted on the SAME watermark row used for timestamps — entity `MJ: Company Integration Sync Watermarks`, `Direction='Pull'`, but `WatermarkType='ChangeToken'` and `WatermarkValue` = JSON of `Object.fromEntries(rollups)` (a `{partition: hash}` object). `SavePartitionRollups` is an idempotent upsert: loads the Pull watermark, sets WatermarkType='ChangeToken'+WatermarkValue=json+LastSyncAt, else `createWatermark(...,'ChangeToken','Pull',...)` (WatermarkService.ts:159-175). `LoadPartitionRollups` returns the parsed Map ONLY when `existing.WatermarkType === 'ChangeToken'` and value parses to an object; corrupt/old/wrong-type value → empty Map → forces a full reconcile, never a wrong skip (WatermarkService.ts:181-195). `ValidateWatermark` for 'ChangeToken' just checks non-empty length (WatermarkService.ts:100-101).

ORPHAN SWEEP. Runs on `(config.fullSync || partitionReconcile) && fetchedExternalIDs.size > 0 && fetchCompletedCleanly` → `DeleteOrphanedRecords` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1426-1430). So partition mode ALWAYS does hard-delete detection across the full set, even for records inside skipped/unchanged partitions, because the complete ExternalID set was collected during fetch.

WATERMARK RESULT. On a clean partition-reconcile fetch, `result.WatermarkAfter = null` (no timestamp written; the ChangeToken row was already written inside applyViaPartitionReconcile) (IntegrationEngine.ts:1375-1377).

FULLSYNC SOURCE. `config.fullSync` = explicit caller `options.FullSync` (GQL `IntegrationStartSync(fullSync: true)`, default false), else periodic cadence from `CompanyIntegration.Configuration {"fullSyncEvery": N}` — every Nth completed `MJ: Company Integration Runs` (and the first) is a full reconcile (IntegrationEngine.ts:527,541-561; resolver IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:3217,3227).

knownGapsOrBugs: MEMORY/SCALE: partition mode buffers the ENTIRE fetched set (accumulatedMapped) in RAM until the post-loop apply — explicitly documented as NOT for multi-million-row objects (IntegrationEngine.ts:2116-2120). A live test on a huge object would OOM; intended for small/medium watermark-less rosters (tens of thousands).

removed partitions are computed by diffPartitions but never deep-applied (they have no incoming records); hard-deletes are reclaimed only via the separate DeleteOrphanedRecords orphan sweep, which requires fetchCompletedCleanly. A partial fetch performs neither the reconcile nor the orphan sweep, so deletes are not detected that run (by design — avoids deleting live records from an incomplete set).

PARTITION-COLLISION FALSE-SKIP RISK (subtle): the rollup is keyed by partition (a hash bucket of ExternalID mod partitionCount), not by record. If two records in the same bucket change such that their combined sorted content-hash set is unchanged it would false-skip — but since each record's content hash is included and sorted, this requires a genuine hash collision; practically negligible but not a record-level guarantee. Lower partitionCount increases records-per-bucket and the blast radius of any single change (a change forces re-apply of the whole bucket), trading skip-granularity for fewer stored rollups.

config.fullSync overrides the stored snapshot to empty (treats all as added) — this is intentional drift-repair, but it means a fullSync ALWAYS re-applies (match+upsert) every record in partition mode, defeating the skip optimization for that run. The orphan sweep also runs. Expensive but correct.

ChangeToken WatermarkValue is an unbounded JSON object of up-to-partitionCount entries (default 256 × ~70 chars ≈ 18KB); if partitionCount is set very large this column could grow significantly. No size guard in SavePartitionRollups.

LoadPartitionRollups silently treats any non-ChangeToken watermark type or unparseable value as an empty snapshot → forces a full reconcile. Switching an existing timestamp-watermark map to partitionReconcile will therefore do one full re-apply on first partition run (no prior ChangeToken row), which is correct but not an incremental.

The sync.partition.reconcile event is only emitted on a clean apply that reaches the end of applyViaPartitionReconcile; a SchemaNotGeneratedError early-return emits NO reconcile event (only a CONFIGURATION_ERROR in result.Errors), so a test waiting on the event would hang in that failure mode.

result.subsystem: keyset-restart (KEYSET pagination + RESTART resilience) in the MemberJunction Integration sync engine
result.mechanics:
There are TWO orthogonal restart/resume mechanisms, plus a third that is currently dormant.

(1) KEYSET (seek) resume — connector opt-in via StableOrderingKey. In ProcessPullSync, the engine computes `isKeysetConnector = config.connector.StableOrderingKey(entityMap.ExternalObjectName) != null` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1096). The base implementation returns null (BaseIntegrationConnector.ts:380), so this is OFF for every shipped connector — NO connector in packages/Integration/connectors/src overrides it; only the unit-test mock returns 'hs_object_id' (IntegrationEngine.test.ts:536). When ON: declaring a key forces `initialWatermark = null` (timestamp + keyset are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE per the documented KNOWN LIMITATION at IntegrationEngine.ts:1085-1095). On a non-full incremental, if the loaded Pull watermark has `WatermarkType==='Cursor'` and a non-empty `WatermarkValue`, the engine sets `resumeAfterKey = watermark.WatermarkValue`, emits `sync.resume.keyset` (logger event, IntegrationEngine.ts:1117), and seeds the first FetchContext.AfterKeyValue with it (currentAfterKey init at :1151, passed into ctx.AfterKeyValue at :1183). The connector is contracted to fetch `WHERE <key> > AfterKeyValue ORDER BY <key>` and return the new high-water key in `FetchBatchResult.NextAfterKeyValue` (BaseIntegrationConnector.ts:126,164-165); the engine advances `currentAfterKey = batch.NextAfterKeyValue ?? currentAfterKey` (:1342). Resume is no-dup/no-gap because the seek is strictly `> AfterKey` and the idempotent upsert + content-hash absorb any boundary overlap (:1080-1081).

(2) CHECKPOINT cadence. Every 25 batches (`batchCount % 25 === 0`, IntegrationEngine.ts:1347) the engine does two things: (a) emits a durable `checkpoint` progress-artifact event via `logger?.checkpoint(stage, {watermark, afterKey, page, offset, cursor, batchIndex, recordsInMap})` (:1348-1356) — this lands as eventType `'checkpoint'` carrying `resumableState` (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:151-153, types.ts:54/119-124); and (b) if keyset, durably persists the seek key with `watermarkService.SaveKeysetPosition(entityMapID, currentAfterKey, contextUser)` (:1361-1362) — the durable floor for a hard SIGKILL between graceful checkpoints, costing at most ~25 batches of re-fetch.

(3) WatermarkType='Cursor' hold at interruption. End-of-loop disposition (IntegrationEngine.ts:1375-1420): a CLEAN keyset scan calls `ClearKeysetPosition` which keeps WatermarkType='Cursor' but NULLs WatermarkValue (so next run seeks fresh; result.WatermarkAfter=null; :1378-1384, WatermarkService.ts:141-151). An EARLY-EXIT keyset scan (abort/fetch-error/safety-limit/duplicate-batch — all flip `fetchCompletedCleanly=false`) with a currentAfterKey calls `SaveKeysetPosition` (:1415-1419), which on an existing watermark sets WatermarkType='Cursor', WatermarkValue=afterKey, LastSyncAt=now (WatermarkService.ts:116-133), or creates a new Cursor watermark via the shared `createWatermark`. So an interrupted keyset scan HOLDS its AfterKey on a Cursor-typed Pull watermark; a clean scan NULLS it.

(4) ORPHANED-RUN detection + resume on restart. `ResumeOrphanedSyncs(contextUser, provider)` (IntegrationEngine.ts:171) is fired once at MJAPI startup (MJServer/src/index.ts:965, fire-and-forget, system user). It RunViews `MJ: Company Integration Runs` with `ExtraFilter: Status='In Progress'` (:176-180) — a run is set to `Status='In Progress'` at start (:581) and flipped to 'Success'/'Failed' by FinalizeRun (:2951) / FailRun (:2973), so any still-'In Progress' row is orphaned by a crash. Logs `No orphaned syncs to resume` (:183) or `Found N orphaned sync(s) to resume` (:187). For each: it loads completed entity maps from `MJ: Company Integration Run Details` (a run-detail row per finished map, written by CreateRunDetail at end of ProcessPullSync :1432), builds a lowercased completedEntityIDs set keyed on detail.EntityID (:202-207), filters config.entityMaps to only the REMAINING maps (:217-219), and re-runs them on the SAME run record via ExecuteEntityMaps→FinalizeRun (:235-237). If all maps were already done it marks the run Success (:222-225). A failure during resume sets Status='Failed' + ErrorLog so it isn't re-picked (:248-252). Note: orphan-resume does NOT re-seed AfterKey directly — it relies on each resumed map re-loading its persisted Cursor watermark inside ProcessPullSync to continue the seek.

NOT to be confused: DeleteOrphanedRecords (:1964) is delete-detection (MJ records whose ExternalID wasn't in fetchedExternalIDs), unrelated to orphaned-RUN resume; it emits the ORPHANS_DETECTED warning. Partition (Merkle) reconcile (WatermarkType='ChangeToken') is a third mode, mutually exclusive with keyset.

knownGapsOrBugs: KEYSET RESUME IS DORMANT IN PRODUCTION: no shipped connector overrides StableOrderingKey (base returns null at BaseIntegrationConnector.ts:380; grep of packages/Integration/connectors/src found zero non-null overrides). The entire keyset/Cursor-AfterKey path (resumeAfterKey, sync.resume.keyset, SaveKeysetPosition, ClearKeysetPosition) is exercised ONLY by the unit-test mock returning 'hs_object_id'. A live test cannot trigger it without a purpose-built connector that declares a StableOrderingKey. The HubSpot comment at IntegrationEngine.ts:1092-1094 explicitly says HubSpot CRM objects deliberately return null and do keyset connector-locally inside the date filter, NOT through this engine path.

DOCUMENTED MUTUAL-EXCLUSION LIMITATION: declaring a StableOrderingKey unconditionally forces initialWatermark=null (IntegrationEngine.ts:1114), so a connector whose object DOES have a usable timestamp incremental would silently turn every incremental into a full scan if it also declares a key. Watermark (what-to-fetch) and keyset (where-am-I) are orthogonal but the engine treats them as exclusive (comment :1085-1095). Future shape = two independent signals; not yet built.

ORPHAN-RESUME GRANULARITY IS PER-ENTITY-MAP, NOT PER-BATCH: ResumeOrphanedSyncs only skips entity maps that have a Run Detail row (i.e. fully completed maps). A map interrupted MID-scan is re-run from its watermark/cursor — fine for keyset (Cursor watermark held) and timestamp (safe-floor), but a non-keyset, non-watermark map that was 80% through re-fetches everything (content-hash skips re-writes but re-fetches). The durable checkpoint resumableState (batchIndex/page/offset) is written to the artifact but ResumeOrphanedSyncs does NOT read it back to resume mid-map — it is surfaced over GQL (ResumableStateJSON) but not consumed by the engine's restart path.

CHECKPOINT CADENCE IS HARD-CODED to every 25 batches (IntegrationEngine.ts:1347) and the durable SaveKeysetPosition only fires inside that same %25 block — so a SIGKILL can cost up to ~25 batches of re-fetch on a keyset map; not configurable.

ResumeOrphanedSyncs is fire-and-forget at startup (MJServer/src/index.ts:965, .catch only logs a warn) and runs as the system user; if it throws before the per-run try/catch (e.g. LoadRunConfiguration failure outside the loop) the orphaned run could remain 'In Progress'. Within the loop, failures correctly flip to 'Failed' to avoid re-pick.

result.mechanics:
DAG/dependency layering controls the ORDER and CONCURRENCY in which a sync run processes its entity maps, and surfaces structured warnings when ordering can't be guaranteed or when a dependent object comes up empty. Two distinct code paths exist, both keyed on IntegrationObjectField.RelatedIntegrationObjectID (the FK edge between IntegrationObjects):

(1) RUNTIME LAYERING during a sync run — IntegrationEngine.ExecuteEntityMaps (IntegrationEngine.ts:602). It calls buildEntityMapDependencyLayers(config, logger) (IntegrationEngine.ts:759) to group the run's entity maps into dependency layers. The graph is built by computeSelectedDependencyGraph(config) (IntegrationEngine.ts:815): it resolves each entity map to an IntegrationObject by matching entityMap.ExternalObjectName (lowercased) to IO.Name, then for each SELECTED IO collects its SELECTED parent IO IDs via IntegrationObjectField.RelatedIntegrationObjectID (only parents that are themselves selected this run count as edges; self-references and unselected parents are dropped — IntegrationEngine.ts:833-837). Layering is Kahn's algorithm (IntegrationEngine.ts:768-786): layer 0 = IOs whose selected parents are all already placed (i.e. roots), layer N depends only on layers < N. Maps with no resolvable IO are treated as roots (layer 0, IntegrationEngine.ts:791). Layers are returned sorted ascending; original config order is preserved within each layer (stable). Layers run SEQUENTIALLY (the `for (const layer of layers)` loop at IntegrationEngine.ts:730) — a child layer never starts until its parent layer fully completes — guaranteeing parent-before-child.

(2) PER-LAYER CONCURRENCY — within a single layer, maps are mutually independent and run via RunAdaptive(layer, fn, concController) (IntegrationEngine.ts:737, AdaptiveConcurrency.ts:126). The concurrency cap is an AIMD controller (AdaptiveConcurrencyController, AdaptiveConcurrency.ts:33): start = getSyncConcurrency(config) (read from CompanyIntegration.Configuration JSON key `syncConcurrency`, default 1, clamped [1,16] — IntegrationEngine.ts:894-904); min=1; max = Math.max(start, connector.MaxConcurrencyHint ?? start) (IntegrationEngine.ts:712-713). OnSuccess adds +1 (additive increase, clamped to max); OnThrottleOrError halves the cap (multiplicative decrease, floored at min). With default syncConcurrency=1 and no MaxConcurrencyHint, min=max=1 → strictly sequential (unchanged legacy behavior). Note: a plain map data failure reports `{ ok, throttled: false }` (IntegrationEngine.ts:744) so a transform/FK/validation failure does NOT cut the cap — only a real throttle does, and the per-map success path ramps the cap UP. HubSpotConnector overrides MaxConcurrencyHint => 4 (HubSpotConnector.ts:1081), so a HubSpot run starting at syncConcurrency=1 will ramp up to 4 in-flight per layer on clean maps.

WARNINGS — three distinct codes:
- DEPENDENCY_LAYERING_DEGRADED (stage 'dependency-graph'): emitted by buildEntityMapDependencyLayers in TWO sub-cases. (a) Kahn detects a cycle/unresolved parent — when no IO is `ready` but `remaining` is non-empty, it dumps the rest into the current layer and warns with data { unresolvedObjectCount: N } (IntegrationEngine.ts:774-782). (b) the whole graph build throws — caught, runs all maps in a single flat layer, warns with data { fallback: 'single-layer' } (IntegrationEngine.ts:796-803). Both mean parent-before-child is NOT guaranteed for the affected objects.
- SECOND_LAYER_EMPTY (stage = entityMap.ExternalObjectName): emitted by checkSecondLayerEmpty (IntegrationEngine.ts:851), called AFTER each map completes (IntegrationEngine.ts:660). It maintains processedByIoId (running record count per IO). An object is "second-layer" if the FK graph knows it has parents OR its IntegrationObject.Category === 'Association' (IntegrationEngine.ts:870 — the Category branch catches HubSpot associations that carry NO FK edges). If a second-layer object has RecordsProcessed === 0 (IntegrationEngine.ts:872) it warns, with data { parentRecordCounts: {parentName: count}, parentsHadRows: boolean, category }. The message text branches on whether there were known parents and whether parents had rows (IntegrationEngine.ts:879-883).
- ZERO_PARENTS (stage = the association object's ExternalObjectName): NOT emitted by the engine — emitted by HubSpotConnector.FetchAssociationChanges (HubSpotConnector.ts:3171) as a connector-level FetchWarning when on the FIRST page (parentOffset===0) there are zero parent IDs in MJ to read associations from. data = { object, parentType }. The engine forwards ANY connector FetchWarning at IntegrationEngine.ts:1236-1240 via logger.warning(entityMap.ExternalObjectName ?? 'sync', w.Code, w.Message, w.Data).

WARNING TRANSPORT/SURFACE: All three go through SyncLogger.warning(stage, code, message, data) (SyncLogger.ts:137) which emits a 'sync.warning' SyncLogEvent to console.warn AND forwards to the durable progress artifact via emitter.warning(stage, code, message, warningData) (SyncLogger.ts:192-198). The emitter writes a progress.jsonl event with eventType='warning' and data={ code, ...data } (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:136-142), pushing onto an in-memory warnings[] with warningCount (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:104-105, 227-228). The reader reconstructs SyncWarning {code, stage, message, data} from each eventType==='warning' line (IntegrationProgressReader.ts:146-169), populating snapshot.warnings[] and snapshot.warningCount. The GQL resolver toRunSummaryArtifact (IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4115) exposes these as WarningCount (int) and Warnings (string[]), formatted as `[CODE] stage: message` (IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4134). Warnings NEVER fail the run (SyncWarning doc, types.ts:83).

(3) METADATA-LEVEL DEPENDENCY ORDER (separate, not used by the run loop) — IntegrationEngineBase.GetObjectsInDependencyOrder(integrationID) (IntegrationEngineBase.ts:234) topologically sorts ALL active IntegrationObjects (filtered Status==='Active', IntegrationEngineBase.ts:220-224) by the same RelatedIntegrationObjectID FK edges, ties broken by IntegrationObject.Sequence, cycles fall back to Sequence order (IntegrationEngineBase.ts:277-285). Re-exported on the engine facade at IntegrationEngine.ts:3195. This is a metadata-ordering helper; the live run uses buildEntityMapDependencyLayers, not this.

knownGapsOrBugs: Layering uses two SEPARATE topological-sort implementations that can disagree. The live run uses buildEntityMapDependencyLayers (IntegrationEngine.ts:759, selected-objects-only, ties by config order) while GetObjectsInDependencyOrder (IntegrationEngineBase.ts:234, ALL active objects, ties by IntegrationObject.Sequence) is the metadata helper. A live test asserting run order must target the RUN events, not GetObjectsInDependencyOrder — they can produce different orderings.

ZERO_PARENTS (connector-emitted) and SECOND_LAYER_EMPTY (engine-emitted) can BOTH fire for the same empty association object in one run: HubSpot emits ZERO_PARENTS from FetchAssociationChanges, and the engine independently emits SECOND_LAYER_EMPTY in checkSecondLayerEmpty for the same map (Category==='Association', RecordsProcessed===0). So a single empty HubSpot association may produce two warnings/double-count WarningCount.

Cycle handling DIFFERS between the two sorts: buildEntityMapDependencyLayers (run path) places ALL remaining cyclic IOs into the CURRENT layer and stops (IntegrationEngine.ts:778-782) — so objects in later real layers may be collapsed up and parent-before-child is lost; it warns DEPENDENCY_LAYERING_DEGRADED. GetObjectsInDependencyOrder appends leftovers in Sequence order and breaks (IntegrationEngineBase.ts:277-285) with NO warning. The metadata helper silently degrades on cycles.

getSyncConcurrency only reads CompanyIntegration.Configuration.syncConcurrency; the per-layer cap can still ramp ABOVE that via MaxConcurrencyHint (HubSpot=4) regardless of the configured value — a test setting syncConcurrency=1 on HubSpot will still see up to 4 in-flight. There is no config knob to cap below the hint other than the hint itself.

The DEPENDENCY_LAYERING_DEGRADED build-failure branch (catch at IntegrationEngine.ts:796) is hard to trigger in a live test — it requires computeSelectedDependencyGraph itself to throw, which only happens on an unexpected runtime error, not on ordinary cyclic/unmapped data. The cycle sub-case (unresolvedObjectCount) is the testable one.

computeSelectedDependencyGraph resolves entity maps to IntegrationObjects by lowercased ExternalObjectName === IO.Name. If an entity map's ExternalObjectName drifts from the IO.Name (e.g. plural/singular or display-name mismatch), the map silently becomes a layer-0 root (IntegrationEngine.ts:791) with no warning, defeating ordering — a latent silent-misorder not surfaced by any warning code.

result.subsystem: conflict-merge (bidirectional 3-way field-level merge + conflict resolution on push)
result.mechanics:
The conflict-merge logic lives entirely on the PUSH (MJ→external, "DestToSource") path, gated by the entity map's per-record 3-way combine. It is NOT the read-side MatchEngine path (which only does a coarse `Manual`→Skip on pull, MatchEngine.ts:108-110).

PUSH ENTRY: `SyncPushDirection` selects `pushFieldMaps = fieldMaps.filter(fm => fm.Direction === 'DestToSource' || fm.Direction === 'Both')` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1504-1506; empty → entity push skipped, returns EmptyResult). Changed records come from `LoadChangedMJRecords` (incremental, via Record Changes since the Push watermark, excluding the System user `ECAFCCEC-6A37-EF11-86D4-000D3A4E707E`, IntegrationEngine.ts:1561-1567) or `LoadAllMJRecords` (full). Each change's `Fields` is the FULL MJ row loaded via `entity.GetAll()` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1600) — so it INCLUDES the `__mj_integration_*` mirror columns. `PushSingleRecord` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1662) builds `externalAttributes` from `fm.DestinationFieldName`→`fm.SourceFieldName`, then for an EXISTING external record (has externalID) calls `computePushCombine` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1726).

3-WAY COMBINE (`computePushCombine`, IntegrationEngine.ts:1831-1912): base/ancestor = `JSON.parse(change.Fields['__mj_integration_LastSyncedSnapshot'])`; ours = `change.Fields[fm.DestinationFieldName]` (the MJ column); theirs = `ext.Fields[fm.SourceFieldName]` from a live `connector.GetRecord`. Per field map: `mjChanged = !valuesEqual(mjVal, baseVal)`, `extChanged = !valuesEqual(extVal, baseVal)`. (1) mjChanged & !extChanged → push ours (`toPush[fm.SourceFieldName] = mjVal`). (2) mjChanged & extChanged & values differ → TRUE CONFLICT, push `fm.DestinationFieldName` onto `conflictFields[]`. (3) !mjChanged → nothing pushed; both-changed-same → converged/skipped. `valuesEqual` is loose: `a===b`, both null, else `String(a)===String(b)` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1915-1920).

CONFLICT POLICY (`entityMap.ConflictResolution`, default `'DestWins'` when falsy, IntegrationEngine.ts:1861): `mjWinsConflict = policy === 'DestWins' || (policy === 'MostRecent' && mostRecentWinner !== 'external')`. DestWins or MostRecent-favoring-MJ → push mjVal for the conflicting field; SourceWins or MostRecent-favoring-external → leave external (field omitted from toPush); Manual → set `manualConflict=true`, do NOT push. NOTE the naming inversion: `SourceWins` resolution string is logged as `'external-wins'` and `DestWins` as `'mj-wins'` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1894-1897) — "Source"=external system, "Dest"=MJ.

MOSTRECENT: `resolveMostRecentWinner(change.Fields, ext)` → `mostRecentWinner(mjFields['__mj_UpdatedAt'], ext.ModifiedAt)` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1929-1931, ConflictRecency.ts:33-38). Record-level (not per-field) recency; ties → 'mj'; either timestamp missing/unparseable → null → treated as MJ-wins (DestWins fallback, conflict never dropped).

CONFLICT EVENT + MANUAL QUARANTINE: when `conflictFields.length > 0`, emits `logger.emit('sync.record.conflict', {entity, externalId, mjRecordId, conflictFields, policy, resolution})` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1898-1905). If Manual, calls `markConflictOnMJRecord` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1934-1957) which loads the MJ mirror row and Sets `__mj_integration_SyncStatus='Conflict'` and `__mj_integration_SyncMessage='Bidirectional conflict: external changed <fields> since last sync; awaiting manual resolution.'`, then `combine` returns `{action:'skip'}` → `RecordsSkipped++` (no push). Non-Manual policies still push and the conflict event is informational only.

EMPTY-MERGE: if `toPush` is empty after merge (external already had our change / external-wins took every field), `RecordsSkipped++` and no UpdateRecord call (IntegrationEngine.ts:1733-1738).

SAFE FALLBACKS (all → push the FULL attribute set, prior last-write-wins): snapshot missing/empty/non-string, `JSON.parse` fail, `!connector.SupportsGet`, `GetRecord` throws, or `GetRecord` returns null (IntegrationEngine.ts:1842-1859).

SNAPSHOT WRITE (pull-apply): `SetStandardIntegrationFields` sets `__mj_integration_LastSyncedSnapshot = JSON.stringify(record.MappedFields)`, `__mj_integration_SyncStatus='Active'`, clears `__mj_integration_SyncMessage` to null, sets `__mj_integration_LastWriterDirection='Pull'` (IntegrationEngine.ts:2774-2820). So a clean pull resets any prior 'Conflict' status.

PUSH_SKIPPED is a SEPARATE warning (not conflict): `warnPushSkip` → `logger.warning(stage, 'PUSH_SKIPPED', ...)` for 403-forbidden writes, unsupported create, and create-with-no-ExternalID (IntegrationEngine.ts:1806-1819). It is NOT emitted by conflict resolution.

GRAPHQL/ARTIFACT SURFACING: SyncLogger forwards `sync.record.conflict` to the durable emitter as a `record.error` event with `level:'warn', message:'conflict'` (SyncLogger.ts:189-191) — it does NOT become a SyncWarning. `sync.warning` (PUSH_SKIPPED) forwards to emitter `warning()` → eventType `'warning'`, payload `data:{code:'PUSH_SKIPPED',...}` (SyncLogger.ts:192-199, IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:136-143), which the reader aggregates into `warnings[]` as SyncWarning `{code,stage,message}`. Over GraphQL: `IntegrationTailRunEvents(runID, sinceSeq)` returns the conflict as an event with `EventType:'record.error'`, `Level:'warn'`, `Message:'conflict'`, and the full `{conflictFields,policy,resolution,...}` JSON in `DataJSON`. PUSH_SKIPPED surfaces both as a `'warning'` event in `IntegrationTailRunEvents` AND in `IntegrationListRuns`/run-summary `Warnings[]` rendered as `"[PUSH_SKIPPED] <stage>: <message>"` (IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4134). The Manual `SyncStatus='Conflict'` + `SyncMessage` land on the mirror DB row, queryable via RunView on the integration table.

knownGapsOrBugs: NAMING INVERSION (intentional but bug-prone for test authors): in ConflictResolution, 'Source' = the EXTERNAL system and 'Dest' = MJ. So SourceWins → external wins (logged 'external-wins'), DestWins → MJ wins (logged 'mj-wins'). A test asserting 'SourceWins pushes MJ value' would be WRONG — SourceWins OMITS the MJ change. Default when ConflictResolution is falsy is 'DestWins' (MJ wins), IntegrationEngine.ts:1861.

computePushCombine reads ours from change.Fields[fm.DestinationFieldName] but the snapshot ancestor is JSON.stringify(record.MappedFields) written on pull (SetStandardIntegrationFields:2782). MappedFields is keyed by MJ destination column names, so base[fm.DestinationFieldName] aligns — but ONLY if the pull writes the same field-map destination keys. If a mirror table column was added later (schema evolution) or a field map changed between snapshot-write and push, base[DestinationFieldName] is undefined → that field reads as mjChanged whenever non-null → spurious 'only-we-changed' push. No version/shape guard on the snapshot JSON.

MostRecent is RECORD-level, not field-level: a single newer-timestamp side wins EVERY conflicting field, even fields that side didn't touch most recently. Comment at ConflictRecency.ts:6-7 acknowledges this ('most sources don't expose per-field timestamps').

MostRecent indeterminate (null timestamp) silently degrades to DestWins/MJ-wins with NO warning or event flag distinguishing it from a real MostRecent→MJ decision. resolution logs 'mj-wins (most-recent)' identically whether recency was computed or fell back. A live test cannot tell 'MJ genuinely newer' from 'timestamp missing' via the event payload.

The conflict event is forwarded to the durable artifact as eventType 'record.error' (level 'warn', message 'conflict'), NOT as a 'warning'/SyncWarning (SyncLogger.ts:189-191). Therefore conflicts do NOT appear in the run-summary Warnings[]/WarningCount rollup — only PUSH_SKIPPED-style warnings do. To assert a conflict over GraphQL you must page IntegrationTailRunEvents and inspect EventType='record.error' + DataJSON, not the summary warnings.

markConflictOnMJRecord is entirely best-effort (try/catch swallow, IntegrationEngine.ts:1954-1956) and only acts if the entity has __mj_integration_SyncStatus / __mj_integration_SyncMessage columns. A __mj-target entity or a table predating these columns gets a silently-undocumented Manual conflict — quarantined (not pushed) but with no DB-visible Conflict marker. The conflict event still fires, but there is no persistent queryable conflict state.

valuesEqual uses String(a)===String(b), so 1 vs '1', true vs 'true', and JSON-shaped values that stringify identically are treated as equal — a real type-coercion change between sides can be masked (no conflict detected, change dropped). Conversely objects/arrays compare by String() ('[object Object]') and can false-converge.

Manual on the PULL/read side (MatchEngine.ResolveExistingRecord:108) maps an existing matched record to ChangeType='Skip' — i.e. inbound external changes to an already-synced record are NEVER applied under Manual, with no Conflict marker on that path (distinct, coarser behavior than the push-side field-level Manual quarantine). Easy to conflate the two Manual behaviors.

result.subsystem: delete-tombstone (DELETE detection + tombstoning in the Integration pull-sync engine)
result.mechanics:
DELETE DETECTION HAS TWO INDEPENDENT PATHS with different behavior:

(1) ORPHAN SWEEP (full-sync / partition-reconcile delete-detection). After the fetch loop, `ProcessPullSync` calls `DeleteOrphanedRecords` ONLY when `(config.fullSync || partitionReconcile) && fetchedExternalIDs.size > 0 && fetchCompletedCleanly` (IntegrationEngine.ts:1426-1430). `fetchedExternalIDs` is a `Set<string>` accumulated from every `rec.ExternalID` across all batches (line 1156, 1264-1266). `fetchCompletedCleanly` starts `true` (line 1154) and is flipped to `false` on ANY of: abort signal (1162), MAX_BATCHES_PER_MAP=5000 safety limit (1171), FetchChanges throw (1218), SchemaNotGeneratedError on apply (1302), duplicate-batch fingerprint loop guard (1258). This is the CRITICAL guard — a partial/errored/aborted fetch never triggers orphan deletion, so live records aren't wrongly tombstoned from an incomplete upstream read. `DeleteOrphanedRecords` (1964-2024) loads ALL `MJ: Company Integration Record Maps` rows for this CompanyIntegrationID+EntityID, filters to orphans = map rows whose `ExternalSystemRecordID` is NOT in `fetchedExternalIDs` (line 1984). If orphans>0 it emits the `ORPHANS_DETECTED` warning (1992-1997) with `warningData = { orphanCount: N }`, then for each orphan loads the MJ entity by PK and calls `entity.Delete()` directly (line 2011), incrementing `result.RecordsDeleted` on success.

(2) EXPLICIT DELETE ChangeType (push/incremental path). `DeleteRecord` (2540-2588) honors `entityMap.DeleteBehavior`: `'DoNothing'` returns false (no-op, 2550); `'SoftDelete'` (2566-2580) does NOT delete the row — it loads it and sets `__mj_integration_SyncStatus='Archived'`, `__mj_integration_LastSyncedAt=now`, `__mj_integration_IsTombstoned=true`, `__mj_integration_DeletedDetectedAt=now` (ISO string), then `entity.Save()`; `'HardDelete'` (the fall-through, 2582) physically `entity.Delete()`s the row.

KEY DISCREPANCY (likely bug, see gaps): the ORPHAN SWEEP path (path 1) ALWAYS hard-deletes via `entity.Delete()` and IGNORES `DeleteBehavior` entirely AND never sets the tombstone columns (`__mj_integration_IsTombstoned`/`__mj_integration_DeletedDetectedAt`). Only path (2) — the Delete-ChangeType — sets tombstones / respects SoftDelete. So an upstream hard-delete discovered by the full-sync orphan sweep is physically removed regardless of an entity map configured `DeleteBehavior='SoftDelete'`, and no tombstone is recorded.

TOMBSTONE LIFECYCLE / CLEARING: On every successful pull-apply (create/update), `SetStandardIntegrationFields` resets `__mj_integration_IsTombstoned` to `false` (line 2824-2826) — a record reappearing upstream is un-tombstoned (DeletedDetectedAt is NOT re-cleared there, only IsTombstoned). Tombstone columns are generated by SchemaBuilder/DDLGenerator on every integration mirror table: SQL Server `__mj_integration_IsTombstoned BIT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` + `__mj_integration_DeletedDetectedAt DATETIMEOFFSET NULL`; Postgres `BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` + `TIMESTAMPTZ NULL` (DDLGenerator.ts:276-277, 290-291). All Set() calls are guarded by `hasField(...)` so they no-op on tables predating the columns.

SCHEDULED FULL RECONCILE: incrementals normally skip orphan detection; an integration opts into periodic hard-delete reclamation via `CompanyIntegration.Configuration` JSON `{"fullSyncEvery": N}` — every Nth completed run (and the first) runs as a full sync (resolveScheduledFullSync, 541-...).

COUNTS/SURFACING: `result.RecordsDeleted` is incremented for both archived (SoftDelete) and hard-deleted records (2013, 2318, 2331 picks the 'archived' vs 'deleted' outcome label). It flows into JSONL events `sync.record.archived`/`sync.record.saved` (outcome field, 2331-2340), `sync.entity-map.complete` (recordsDeleted, 656), and `sync.run.complete` (recordsDeleted, 393). The GQL run-counts type `IntegrationRunCountsOutput` exposes only Processed/Succeeded/Failed/Skipped/TotalKnown (resolver 735-741) — the artifact reader aggregates only those four (IntegrationProgressReader.ts:138-141), so RecordsDeleted is NOT a GQL Counts field. The ONLY GQL-visible orphan signal is the `ORPHANS_DETECTED` warning surfaced via `Warnings: ["[ORPHANS_DETECTED] <stage>: ..."]` + `WarningCount` on IntegrationRunSummaryArtifactOutput, and the raw events via IntegrationTailRunEvents (`sync.warning` with DataJSON.code=ORPHANS_DETECTED + warningData.orphanCount; `sync.record.archived` events).

knownGapsOrBugs: ORPHAN SWEEP IGNORES DeleteBehavior: DeleteOrphanedRecords (IntegrationEngine.ts:2011) calls entity.Delete() unconditionally — it does NOT honor entityMap.DeleteBehavior. A full-sync-detected upstream hard delete physically removes the MJ row even when DeleteBehavior='SoftDelete' or 'DoNothing'. Only the Delete-ChangeType path (DeleteRecord) respects DeleteBehavior. A live test on a SoftDelete-configured map will see physical deletion (not archival) from the orphan path.

ORPHAN SWEEP NEVER SETS TOMBSTONE COLUMNS: because it hard-deletes, __mj_integration_IsTombstoned / __mj_integration_DeletedDetectedAt are never set for orphan-detected deletions — the queryable tombstone only exists for the SoftDelete Delete-ChangeType path. So 'find records deleted upstream' via IsTombstoned=true MISSES every full-sync orphan deletion.

RecordsDeleted IS NOT PERSISTED / NOT IN GQL COUNTS: CreateRunDetail writes only 'Processed:N' (no deleted column), run.TotalRecords excludes orphan deletes, and IntegrationRunCountsOutput/IntegrationProgressReader aggregate only processed/succeeded/failed/skipped. The delete count is recoverable only from the raw JSONL events' DataJSON.recordsDeleted or by counting ORPHANS_DETECTED.orphanCount — there is no first-class queryable delete metric.

DeletedDetectedAt NOT CLEARED ON REVIVAL: SetStandardIntegrationFields (2824) resets IsTombstoned=false when a record reappears but leaves __mj_integration_DeletedDetectedAt populated, so a revived record retains a stale deletion timestamp (IsTombstoned is the authoritative live/dead flag; DeletedDetectedAt is a one-way stamp).

ORPHAN DELETION USES entity.Delete() PER-RECORD WITH NO CASCADE/FK GUARD BEYOND BaseEntity: failures are only console.warn'd (2017,2021) and do NOT increment RecordsErrored or fail the run — a blocked orphan delete (FK constraint) is silently skipped, so the MJ row survives while the map row remains, and it will be re-flagged as an orphan every subsequent full sync.

ORPHAN SWEEP DELETES THE MJ ROW BUT DOES NOT REMOVE THE Company Integration Record Map ENTRY: only the entity row is deleted; the record-map row persists, so on the next full sync the same (now-already-deleted) external ID is re-detected as an orphan and InnerLoad returns not-loaded → logged 'already deleted from MJ' and skipped (harmless but the map row is never reaped).

result.mechanics:
PER-RECORD FAILURE ISOLATION IS ASYMMETRIC BETWEEN PULL AND PUSH, AND THE PULL PATH IS NOT PER-RECORD ISOLATED — IT IS BATCH-LEVEL ALL-OR-NOTHING. This is the documented grace gap.

PULL path (external -> MJ) — the dominant write path. `ProcessPullSync` fetches batches and calls `ApplyRecords()` (IntegrationEngine.ts:2201). ApplyRecords chunks the resolved records into sub-batches of `APPLY_BATCH_SIZE = 500` (line 2214). For EACH sub-batch it does: `provider.BeginTransaction()` (2230), then a `for` loop calling `ApplySingleRecord` per record inside a single `try` (2231-2235), then `provider.CommitTransaction()` (2236). If ANY single record throws inside that loop, control jumps to `catch (err)` (2237) which calls `provider.RollbackTransaction()` (2238) — undoing EVERY record already saved in that 500-record batch — then restores the in-memory counters to their pre-batch snapshot (2241-2244) and marks ALL records in the batch as errored: `for (const rec of batch) { result.RecordsProcessed++; result.RecordsErrored++; result.Errors.push(...) }` (2266-2277), each with `ErrorMessage: 'Batch rolled back: <msg>'` (2272) and the SAME ClassifyError code/severity for the whole batch (2273-2274). So one bad value/FK/validation failure sinks up to 500 good records. This is asserted by the unit test at IntegrationEngine.test.ts:348-355 ("a single failing record rolls back every record in the batch ... All 3 records are reported as errored ... `result.Errors.every(e => e.ErrorMessage.startsWith('Batch rolled back:'))`").

What makes a single record throw (poisoning its batch): CreateRecord throws `Failed to create <entity> record: <CompleteMessage>` when `entity.Save()` returns false (2362-2368); UpdateRecord throws `Failed to update <entity> record <id>: <msg>` (2445-2451); validateEntity throws `Validation failed for <entity>: <messages>` (2531-2534); SaveRecordMap throws `Failed to save record map for external ID <id>: <msg>` (2870-2873). All of these escape ApplySingleRecord -> the batch try/catch -> rollback. NOTE Delete is the one exception: DeleteRecord returns a boolean (does NOT throw on blocked delete); a failed delete increments RecordsErrored for that one record WITHOUT rolling back the batch (ApplySingleRecord 2316-2321, DeleteRecord 2582-2587) — so deletes ARE effectively per-record-isolated, creates/updates/validation are NOT.

SchemaNotGeneratedError is a special FAIL-STOP-FOR-THE-WHOLE-OBJECT case: when Save() fails because spCreate/spUpdate/spDelete doesn't exist (detectSchemaNotGenerated matches `Could not find stored procedure '...'` for SQL Server or `function ...(...) does not exist` for PG, lines 105-110), the error is re-thrown out of ApplyRecords (2250-2252), caught in ProcessPullSync (1283-1305), which marks the REMAINING records as Skipped (not errored), pushes ONE error with ErrorCode `CONFIGURATION_ERROR`/Severity `Critical` (1291-1297), sets `hasMore=false` and aborts all further fetches for that entityMap.

PUSH path (MJ -> external) IS genuinely per-record isolated: `ProcessPushSync` loops `for (const change of changedRecords)` with a per-record `try/catch` around `PushSingleRecord` (1516-1535); a failure increments RecordsErrored by 1, pushes one error (ErrorCode `CONNECTOR_ERROR`, Severity `Critical`, ExternalID = change.RecordID), and the loop CONTINUES to the next record. No transaction, no batch rollback.

ENTITY-MAP-LEVEL isolation also exists: in ExecuteEntityMaps, each map runs inside `processOne` with its own try/catch (641-697); a thrown error from one entity map increments aggregate.RecordsErrored by 1, pushes one error (ErrorCode `CONNECTOR_ERROR`, ChangeType `Skip`), and the run proceeds to the next map. So a whole-object failure does not sink other objects.

VALUE-COERCION GRACE (the layer that PREVENTS most per-record failures): FieldMappingEngine transform steps default `OnError = 'Null'` (FieldMappingEngine.ts:125, HandleTransformError 170-179) — a bad coercion nulls THAT FIELD and lets the record sync, rather than failing the record. Coerce/format throw controlled errors on unparseable number/date (lines 237,244,275,290) that are caught and nulled. And SetEntityFields/coerceIncomingValue converts empty strings to null before BaseEntity.Set so a "" never hits a DECIMAL/INT/DATE column. So most "bad value" cases never reach the batch-rollback path at all — they're silently nulled. A record only poisons its batch if the bad value survives coercion and BaseEntity.Save()/Validate() rejects it (FK violation, NOT NULL on a nulled required field, length, validation rule).

WATERMARK SAFE-FLOOR (data-loss prevention tied to the batch rollback): `lastCleanWatermark` only advances on a fully-clean batch (`if (result.RecordsErrored === erroredBeforeApply) lastCleanWatermark = currentWatermark`, line 1337); a rolled-back batch does NOT advance the floor, so its records stay re-fetchable next incremental run (the idempotent upsert + content-hash reconcile them).

PERSISTENCE OF ERRORS: aggregate `result.Errors.slice(0,100)` is written to `CompanyIntegrationRun.ErrorLog` as JSON (FinalizeRun 2952-2953). Run status is set `Failed` if `RecordsErrored > 0`, else `Success` (2951). One `CompanyIntegrationRunDetail` row per entity map (CreateRunDetail 2879-2898) with `RecordID = 'Processed:<N>'`, `IsSuccess = (RecordsErrored === 0)`, `Action = 'INSERT'|'UPDATE'` — there are NO per-record detail rows. Per-record/batch errors are also emitted to the durable JSONL artifact via `logger.emit('sync.record.error', {phase:'save', batchSize, error:'Batch rolled back: ...', errorCode})` — ONE event per batch, not per record (line 2259), and fetch errors emit the same event with `phase:'fetch'` (1212).
