knownGapsOrBugs: THE CORE GRACE GAP (intended/documented, not a code defect): the Pull path is NOT per-record isolated. One record that fails BaseEntity.Save()/Validate() (FK violation, NOT-NULL, length, validation rule, or a SaveRecordMap failure) throws, triggering RollbackTransaction over the WHOLE up-to-500-record sub-batch. Every good record in that batch is reported errored ('Batch rolled back:') and NONE are committed. So a single poison record can fail up to 500 sibling records. ApplyRecords' own comment at 2208-2213 states this explicitly. The method is named/commented 'handling each individually for error isolation' (2198-2199) but the implementation is batch-atomic — the doc comment contradicts the behavior.

ASYMMETRY: Pull (Create/Update) = batch-atomic all-or-nothing; Push = true per-record isolation; Delete (even on Pull) = per-record isolation (returns bool, no rollback); SchemaNotGenerated = fail-stop for the entire object. A live test must pick the right path — asserting 'rest persist' on a Pull Create/Update will FAIL because the batch sinks together.

Counter double-add hazard avoided but subtle: on rollback the code restores counters to the batch-start snapshot (2241-2244) THEN re-increments RecordsProcessed+RecordsErrored per record (2266-2268). Net RecordsProcessed for a failed batch == batch.length (correct), but RecordsCreated/Updated/Deleted that were bumped pre-failure are discarded — so a record that 'succeeded' earlier in the same batch is silently un-counted and effectively errored, matching the DB rollback.

Granularity loss in reporting: all records in a rolled-back batch get the SAME ErrorMessage ('Batch rolled back: <first-failure-msg>') and the SAME ClassifyError Code/Severity. The actual per-record cause is lost for every record except whichever one threw first; downstream code keying on ErrorCode treats e.g. 499 innocent records as the failing record's classification (e.g. FK_CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION).

Run-detail granularity: CompanyIntegrationRunDetail stores ONE row per entity map with RecordID='Processed:N' (2893) — there are NO per-record audit rows. Per-record error detail lives only in CompanyIntegrationRun.ErrorLog (capped at 100 entries, line 2953) and the JSONL artifact (one event per batch). A test cannot query per-record outcomes from RunDetail rows.

ClassifyError is keyword-substring based on the message text (types.ts:48-85), so classification of the batch error depends on the message of whichever record threw first; an unusual message falls through to UNKNOWN_ERROR/Critical. Not exhaustive — e.g. a SQL 'string or binary data would be truncated' maps to nothing specific and becomes UNKNOWN_ERROR.

partition-reconcile path also routes through ApplyRecords (line 2170), so the same 500-record batch-atomic behavior applies there; on any throw it returns without advancing the snapshot (re-reconciles next run).

result.subsystem: rate-concurrency (Integration sync engine: token-bucket rate limiter + AIMD adaptive concurrency + per-connector 429 retry)
result.mechanics:
There are THREE independent layers, all wired in IntegrationEngine.ts. None is configured by a global "syncConcurrency" alone — each has its own control law.

1) PER-REQUEST ADAPTIVE TOKEN-BUCKET RATE LIMITER (RateLimiter.ts). One RateLimiter instance per CompanyIntegration, stored in `IntegrationEngine._rateLimiters: Map<string, RateLimiter>` keyed by CompanyIntegration.ID (NOT IntegrationID — comment at IntegrationEngine.ts:951-954 says limits are per-credential so two companies on the same vendor get independent buckets). Created lazily in getRateLimiter() (IntegrationEngine.ts:956-973): TokensPerSec = connector.RateLimitPolicy.TokensPerSec ?? (BatchRequestWaitTime>0 ? 1000/BatchRequestWaitTime : 10); Burst = max(1, policy.Burst ?? ceil(tokensPerSec)); ThrottleBackoffFactor from policy. The bucket is a classic AIMD/TCP-congestion token bucket: Acquire(key) (RateLimiter.ts:114-127) refills then consumes 1 token, sleeping waitMsForToken() until one is available, returning ms waited. ReportThrottle(key, retryAfterMs?) (RateLimiter.ts:134-142) MULTIPLICATIVELY cuts effectiveRate by ThrottleBackoffFactor (default 0.5 = halve), floored at MinTokensPerSec (default TokensPerSec/20); if retryAfterMs given it sets frozenUntilMs = now+retryAfterMs and ZEROES tokens (honour "do not call until then"). ReportSuccess(key) (RateLimiter.ts:148-152) ADDITIVELY raises effectiveRate by SuccessRampPerCall (default TokensPerSec/10 ≈ 10 successes to fully recover), clamped to the TokensPerSec ceiling. Refill is frozen during a Retry-After window (refill()/accrualStart() RateLimiter.ts:191-211 exclude the frozen interval). The engine calls this.rateLimit(config) (Acquire) before EVERY outbound call — fetch at IntegrationEngine.ts:1198, delete:1707, update:1739, create:1756, GetRecord/combine path:1849. After each it calls reportRateOutcome/reportRateForCrud: clean fetch → ReportSuccess (1200); a fetch error is fed to ReportThrottle ONLY if ClassifyError(err).Code === 'RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' (IntegrationEngine.ts:1210) — a plain data error does NOT back off the rate. For CRUD, reportRateForCrud (993-996): Success → ramp up; StatusCode===429 → back off; any other failure → rate untouched. ExtractRetryAfterMs(error) (connector hook, default undefined at BaseIntegrationConnector.ts:367) is what supplies the freeze duration. **So: a 429 halves the per-request rate and freezes refills; a data error leaves the rate alone.**

2) PER-LAYER AIMD ADAPTIVE CONCURRENCY (AdaptiveConcurrency.ts + IntegrationEngine.ts:705-746). Entity maps are grouped into dependency layers (parents-first). Per run a single AdaptiveConcurrencyController is built: start = getSyncConcurrency() (from CompanyIntegration.Configuration JSON {"syncConcurrency":N}, default 1, clamped [1,16] — getSyncConcurrency() IntegrationEngine.ts:894-904), min=1, max = max(concurrency, connector.MaxConcurrencyHint ?? concurrency) (IntegrationEngine.ts:712-713). Each layer runs through RunAdaptive(layer, fn, controller) (IntegrationEngine.ts:737). The controller's Cap (AdaptiveConcurrency.ts:48) is the live in-flight bound: OnSuccess() raises cap by +1 up to Max (additive increase); OnThrottleOrError() does cap = max(Min, floor(cap/2)) (multiplicative decrease/halving). RunAdaptive (AdaptiveConcurrency.ts:126-166) spawns min(controller.Max, items.length) workers; each worker gates on the LIVE cap before claiming an item (adaptiveWorker:182-200) — if inFlight >= Cap it parks on a setTimeout(0) MACROTASK (deliberately NOT a microtask, comment 190-194: microtask spinning starves in-flight network I/O). **CRITICAL nuance: at the per-LAYER level the engine NEVER reports `throttled:true` for a map failure** — the fn at IntegrationEngine.ts:737-744 returns `{ ok, throttled: false }` always, with the explicit comment (740-743) that a data failure (FK/validation/transform) must NOT cut the in-flight cap; only the per-request RateLimiter handles real 429s, because a per-map 429 signal isn't cleanly available (fetch 429s break the batch loop). So per-layer concurrency ONLY ramps UP on success; a map failure marks ok:false which DOES call OnThrottleOrError() and halves the cap (applyOutcome AdaptiveConcurrency.ts:203-214 halves on `throttled || !ok`). With default syncConcurrency=1 and no MaxConcurrencyHint, min=max=1 → strictly sequential.

3) PER-CONNECTOR HTTP 429 RETRY (NOT engine-level). The engine's RetryRunner.ts (WithRetry, exponential backoff base 1000ms, MaxAttempts 3, MaxBackoffMs 30000, JitterFraction 0.1, retryable on NETWORK_TIMEOUT/RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED/DATABASE_ERROR per IsRetryableError types.ts:43-45) exists but the rate-concurrency hot path does NOT call WithRetry. Instead each REST connector implements its own retry loop inside MakeHTTPRequest (abstract in BaseRESTIntegrationConnector.ts:126). HubSpot (HubSpotConnector.ts:2043-2076): loops attempt 0..effectiveMaxRetries (MAX_RETRIES=5), on response.status===429 computes CalculateRetryDelay (3404-3406: Retry-After header seconds×1000, else exponential min(1000·2^attempt, 30000)), console.warn `[HubSpot] Rate limited (429), retrying in ...`, sleeps, continues; on exhaustion throws `HubSpot 429 rate limit: request failed after N retries (sustained throttling): <url>` — the literal "429"/"rate limit" text is so ClassifyError maps it to RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED and the engine's ExtractRetryAfterMs (HubSpotConnector.ts:1075-1078: returns 10_000ms on /\b429\b|rate.?limit|too many requests/i) backs off the bucket. Salesforce and ~20 other connectors have the identical pattern (status===429 → CalculateRetryDelay → Sleep → retry; exhaust → throw "...failed after N retries").

CONNECTOR DECLARATIONS: ONLY HubSpot overrides the §7 hooks — RateLimitPolicy (TokensPerSec≈10 from MinRequestIntervalMs default 100, Burst:100, ThrottleBackoffFactor:0.5, HubSpotConnector.ts:1065-1072), ExtractRetryAfterMs (→10_000ms, :1075-1078), MaxConcurrencyHint (4, :1081). All other connectors inherit the base defaults (RateLimitPolicy null, ExtractRetryAfterMs undefined, MaxConcurrencyHint null), so for them the bucket rate falls back to BatchRequestWaitTime or 10 tok/s and concurrency stays at syncConcurrency.

EVENT/PERSISTENCE SURFACE: There is NO `external.call.retry` event ever emitted — the string is declared in the IntegrationProgressEventType union (progress-artifacts/src/types.ts:60) but the emitter has externalCallStart/externalCallComplete only (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:155-163), NO externalCallRetry method, and grep finds zero emit('external.call.retry') in source. 429 retries surface ONLY as console.warn lines, not as structured progress events. Warnings ARE structured: SyncLogger.warning(stage,code,message,data) (SyncLogger.ts:137-139) emits 'sync.warning' → forwarded to emitter.warning → persisted as a `'warning'` progress event in progress.jsonl; the reader aggregates them (IntegrationProgressReader.ts:146-158, filter eventType==='warning') into warningCount/warnings, exposed over GQL as IntegrationRunSummaryArtifactOutput.WarningCount and .Warnings (formatted `[CODE] stage: message`, IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4133-4134). But NONE of the rate/concurrency codes emit a warning — the only warning codes here are DEPENDENCY_LAYERING_DEGRADED (layer cycle) and SECOND_LAYER_EMPTY (silent-empty), which are concurrency-adjacent (dependency layering) but not rate-limit signals.

sourceRefs: packages/Integration/engine/src/RetryRunner.ts:16-76 (WithRetry/DEFAULT_RETRY_CONFIG — NOT used on rate hot path)
knownGapsOrBugs: DEAD/UNWIRED EVENT: the 'external.call.retry' progress event type is declared (progress-artifacts/src/types.ts:60) but is never emitted — there is no externalCallRetry() helper on IntegrationProgressEmitter and zero emit('external.call.retry') call sites. A live test that expects to see retry events in IntegrationTailRunEvents will find NONE; 429 retries are only console.warn lines. If the prompt's hypothesis is 'external.call.retry events fire on 429', that is FALSE in current source.

RetryRunner.WithRetry (engine-level exponential-backoff retry) is NOT invoked anywhere on the rate-concurrency fetch/CRUD hot path — grep shows no WithRetry call site in IntegrationEngine.ts. All real 429 retry happens per-connector inside each connector's own MakeHTTPRequest loop, duplicated across ~21 connectors rather than centralized. RetryRunner is effectively unused dead-ish infrastructure for this subsystem.

Per-LAYER adaptive concurrency can NEVER react to a 429 as a throttle: the engine fn hardcodes throttled:false (IntegrationEngine.ts:744) because a fetch 429 breaks the batch loop before a per-map signal exists. So AdaptiveRunResult.throttled is always 0 at the engine layer and the cap only collapses on a generic map FAILURE (ok:false), conflating data errors with throttles at the concurrency layer. The '429 halves the cap while a data error does not' distinction the prompt asks about is TRUE for the per-request RateLimiter but FALSE for the per-layer concurrency controller (there, BOTH a data failure and a 429-failed map equally halve the cap via OnThrottleOrError on !ok; neither is distinguished).

Only HubSpot declares RateLimitPolicy/ExtractRetryAfterMs/MaxConcurrencyHint. Every other connector inherits null/undefined defaults, so for ~20 connectors the per-request bucket falls back to BatchRequestWaitTime-derived or a flat 10 tok/s, ExtractRetryAfterMs returns undefined (no freeze — only the multiplicative ×0.5 cut applies on a 429), and MaxConcurrencyHint is null so concurrency is pinned to syncConcurrency. A live test should run against HubSpot to exercise the full adaptive path.

ClassifyError is purely string-match on the error message (lowercase includes 'rate limit'/'throttl'/'429'). A 429 surfaced as e.g. a JSON body without those tokens in error.message would classify as UNKNOWN_ERROR and would NOT back off the per-request rate (reportRateOutcome at IntegrationEngine.ts:1210 gates on Code==='RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED'). Connectors rely on embedding the literal '429'/'rate limit' text in their thrown message (HubSpot does so deliberately, :2076).

RateLimiter is in-memory per IntegrationEngine instance (singleton process), so the adaptive rate/freeze state is lost on MJAPI restart and is NOT shared across processes — a multi-instance deployment would each maintain independent buckets and could collectively exceed the vendor rate.

(4788).

=== LIFECYCLE CORRECTNESS ASSESSMENT ===

A) CancelSync — arg: companyIntegrationID (single string, NO runID). Calls static IntegrationEngine.CancelSync(id) which lowercases id, looks up _abortControllers Map, and if found calls controller.abort() returning true; returns false if no controller → resolver returns Success:false "No active sync found for this connector" (resolver:3308). On true returns Success:true "Sync cancellation signalled — will stop after current batch completes". DOES it actually stop an in-flight run? Cooperative only: abortSignal.aborted is checked at ExecuteEntityMaps loop boundaries (engine:629 processOne entry, 731 per-layer, 738 per-map in RunAdaptive) and inside fetch loops (1160). It does NOT interrupt an in-flight connector fetch/batch already executing — only prevents the NEXT map/layer. CANCELLED RUN STATUS BUG: on abort, ExecuteEntityMaps sets aggregate.Success=false + aggregate.ErrorMessage='Sync cancelled by user' (engine:733-734) and breaks; executeSyncInternal does NOT throw (no FailRun) — it falls into the SUCCESS branch and calls FinalizeRun (engine:385). FinalizeRun sets run.Status = result.RecordsErrored>0 ? 'Failed':'Success' (engine:2951) — it IGNORES result.Success/ErrorMessage. Aborted maps contribute 0 RecordsErrored, so a cancelled run is persisted as Status='Success'. There is NO 'Cancelled' run status: MJCompanyIntegrationRun.Status is a strict 4-value enum z.union('Failed','In Progress','Pending','Success') (entity_subclasses:9538, nvarchar(20)) — 'Cancelled' would not be a valid value. The durable artifact ALSO mislabels it: finalizeSyncProgress(progress,true,...) → emitter.complete() → exitReason:'completed', success:true (emitter:185-191). The artifact type union includes exitReason:'aborted' (progress-artifacts/types.ts:148) but NO code path ever emits it. So a user-cancelled sync reports Success everywhere.

B) Deactivate / IsActive enforcement — IntegrationDeactivateConnection only does ci.IsActive=false + Save (resolver:2424); IntegrationReactivateConnection only ci.IsActive=true (2447). DOES A DEACTIVATED CONNECTION STILL SYNC? YES. There is NO CompanyIntegration.IsActive gate anywhere on the sync path: RunSync (engine:269), executeSyncInternal (328), and LoadRunConfiguration (engine:473 — loads the CI at :500 but never reads .IsActive) never check it. The ONLY active-gating is at the entity-map level: LoadRunConfiguration filters maps by `SyncEnabled=1 AND Status='Active'` (engine:489). The scheduled-job driver IntegrationSyncScheduledJobDriver.Execute calls RunSync directly with NO IsActive check (driver:59-66). Deactivate also does NOT pause/disable the linked ScheduledJob nor cancel an in-flight run. So an IsActive=false connection still runs to completion when triggered manually (IntegrationStartSync) or by its still-Active schedule.

C) DeleteConnection cascade — TransactionGroup, FK-safe order: nulls CIRun.ScheduledJobRunID (4632) → deletes ScheduledJob + ScheduledJobRun rows whose Configuration LIKE %<id>% and config.CompanyIntegrationID===id (4640-4667) → deletes CompanyIntegrationRunDetail then CompanyIntegrationRun (4669-4687) → per entity map deletes FieldMaps/Watermarks/RecordMaps (4689-4733) → deletes EntityMaps (4735-4739) → deletes EmployeeCompanyIntegration links (4741-4752) → deletes the CompanyIntegration (4754-4756) → tg.Submit (4759). GAPS: (1) ORPHANS THE CREDENTIAL — the linked MJ:Credentials row (ci.CredentialID, set on create at resolver:2256) is NEVER deleted; the only credential.Delete() in the file is the create-rollback helper (resolver:2111), not this cascade. (2) Does NOT stop an in-flight sync — no CancelSync call before deleting; if a sync is running for this CI, deleting run/map rows mid-sync races the engine's own run.Save(). (3) RecordMap query inside the per-em loop filters only by CompanyIntegrationID (4724) (not EntityID) so it re-queries/deletes the same rows every map iteration. (4) deleteData=true is a NO-OP — only LogError "table deletion not yet implemented" (4765), the physical synced tables are NOT dropped.

D) Schedule create/update/disable — IntegrationCreateSchedule resolves ScheduledJobType by DriverClass='IntegrationSyncScheduledJobDriver', creates ScheduledJob with Status='Active', Configuration JSON {CompanyIntegrationID, SyncDirection?, FullSync?}, NextRunAt via CronExpressionHelper, then sets CI.ScheduleEnabled=true/ScheduleType='Cron'/CronExpression (resolver:3425-3450). IntegrationToggleSchedule sets ScheduledJob.Status = enabled?'Active':'Paused' (3502) — DISABLE = 'Paused', and it does NOT touch CI.ScheduleEnabled, so IntegrationGetStatus.ScheduleEnabled/IntegrationListConnections.ScheduleEnabled stay true even after the schedule is Paused (state desync). IntegrationDeleteSchedule unlinks CI (ScheduleEnabled=false,CronExpression=null), nulls FKs, deletes JobRuns+Job in a tg (3514-3595). IntegrationUpdateSchedule recomputes NextRunAt only if cron/timezone changed (3477). Note CI<->ScheduledJob link is loose: CI.ScheduledJobID is set/cleared inconsistently (CreateSchedule never sets CI.ScheduledJobID; DeleteSchedule clears it by querying ScheduledJobID=).

E) Deselect / remove entity maps — IntegrationUpdateEntityMaps(updates:[EntityMapUpdateInput{EntityMapID,SyncDirection?,Priority?,Status?,Configuration?}]) validates SyncDirection∈{Bidirectional,Pull,Push} and Status∈{Active,Inactive} (resolver:833-845,3714-3729); setting Status='Inactive' is the soft "deselect" (LoadRunConfiguration excludes non-Active maps). IntegrationDeleteEntityMaps(entityMapIDs:[String]) hard-deletes in a tg: per map deletes FieldMaps (by EntityMapID), Watermarks (by EntityMapID), RecordMaps (by CI+EntityID — correctly scoped here, unlike DeleteConnection), then the map (3742-3797). On any tg failure returns 'Transaction failed — all deletes rolled back'.

knownGapsOrBugs: CANCEL-STATUS BUG: a user-cancelled sync is recorded as run Status='Success' and artifact exitReason='completed'/success=true. FinalizeRun (engine:2951) keys Status solely off RecordsErrored>0 and ignores aggregate.Success/aggregate.ErrorMessage='Sync cancelled by user'; the abort path returns normally (no FailRun) into the success branch. The CompanyIntegrationRun.Status enum has no 'Cancelled' value (entity_subclasses:9538), and exitReason:'aborted' exists in the artifact type union (types.ts:148) but is emitted by NO code path. There is no way to distinguish a cancelled run from a clean run via GraphQL.

CANCEL IS COOPERATIVE & NON-INTERRUPTING: CancelSync only flips an AbortController; aborted is checked at map/layer/fetch boundaries (engine:629,731,738,1160). It cannot interrupt a connector batch already in flight, and an empty-but-aborted run still counts 'maps processed' toward Success.

DEACTIVATE IS COSMETIC for sync: CompanyIntegration.IsActive=false does NOT prevent syncing. No IsActive gate in RunSync/executeSyncInternal/LoadRunConfiguration or the scheduled-job driver. A deactivated connection still runs via IntegrationStartSync or its still-Active ScheduledJob. Deactivate also does not pause schedules or cancel in-flight runs. Only entity-map-level SyncEnabled=1 AND Status='Active' gates anything.

DELETECONNECTION ORPHANS THE CREDENTIAL: the cascade never deletes the linked MJ:Credentials row (ci.CredentialID), leaving encrypted-credential orphans. The only credential.Delete() is the create-rollback (resolver:2111).
DELETECONNECTION DOES NOT STOP IN-FLIGHT SYNC: no CancelSync invoked before deleting run/map rows; deleting mid-sync races the engine's run.Save() and can leave inconsistent state.
DELETECONNECTION deleteData=true IS A NO-OP: physical synced tables are never dropped — only a LogError 'table deletion not yet implemented' (resolver:4765).

DELETECONNECTION RecordMap query inside the per-entity-map loop filters by CompanyIntegrationID only (resolver:4724), so it re-fetches/deletes the same connector-wide RecordMap set on every map iteration (redundant, O(maps) repeat).

TOGGLESCHEDULE STATE DESYNC: disabling sets ScheduledJob.Status='Paused' but leaves CompanyIntegration.ScheduleEnabled=true and CronExpression set, so IntegrationGetStatus.ScheduleEnabled / IntegrationListConnections.ScheduleEnabled report a paused schedule as enabled.

CI<->ScheduledJob LINK INCONSISTENCY: IntegrationCreateSchedule sets CI.ScheduleEnabled/CronExpression but never sets CI.ScheduledJobID; DeleteSchedule clears CI rows by ScheduledJobID= filter — so the forward link is unreliable; ListSchedules instead matches via Configuration LIKE %CompanyIntegrationID%.
IntegrationDiscoverConnectors hardcodes Connectors[].IsActive=true (resolver:913), ignoring the DB; an inactive Integration still shows IsActive=true.

result.mechanics:
The integration sync engine emits TWO distinct, parallel event vocabularies, and a live test must know which one it is asserting against.

(1) CONSOLE/GREP VOCABULARY — `SyncLogEvent` (SyncLogger.ts:39-61). `SyncLogger.emit(event, data)` writes one JSON line `{ts, event, ciId, integration, runId, ...data}` to console.log/console.error/console.warn (SyncLogger.ts:141-159). These `event` strings are: sync.run.start, sync.config.loaded, sync.connector.built, sync.connector.test, sync.entity-map.start, sync.resume.keyset, sync.partition.reconcile, sync.fetch.batch.start, sync.fetch.batch.complete, sync.record.decision, sync.record.saved, sync.record.error, sync.record.archived, sync.record.conflict, sync.push.candidates, sync.push.record, sync.push.response, sync.entity-map.complete, sync.run.complete, sync.run.fail, sync.run.cancelled, sync.warning. NOTE: sync.connector.test, sync.push.record, sync.push.response, sync.run.cancelled are declared in the union but have NO emit() call in IntegrationEngine.ts (only emitted ones are at lines 339,348,361,387,403,633,647,666,1049,1117,1186,1212,1221,1348(checkpoint),1462/1479/1490,1898,2185,2259,2294,2333 + warning() at 779,800,885,1238,1813,1992). The console field is literally `event` (NOT EventType).

(2) DURABLE/GQL VOCABULARY — `IntegrationProgressEventType` (progress-artifacts/types.ts:18-74). This is the canonical event-type set a LIVE TEST asserts on via GraphQL, where the field is named `EventType` (subscription, IntegrationProgressResolver.ts:91) or `eventType`→`EventType` (polling, IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:771/4097). The full set: run.start, run.complete, run.fail, run.resumed, stage.start, stage.complete, stage.error, records.batch.start, records.batch.complete, record.error, warning, progress.heartbeat, checkpoint, external.call.start, external.call.complete, external.call.retry, transform.applied, discovery.object.added, discovery.field.added, pk.classifier.invoked, pk.classifier.result, entity.generated, entity.skipped-no-pk.

BRIDGE: SyncLogger.forwardToEmitter (SyncLogger.ts:165-220) selectively MIRRORS the console vocabulary into the durable emitter vocabulary — only at stage/batch/error granularity, never per-record. The mapping is: sync.entity-map.start→stageStart (eventType `stage.start`); sync.fetch.batch.complete→emit `records.batch.complete` with counts={processed:recordCount} (ONLY processed is set — succeeded/failed/skipped are absent on batch events); sync.record.error→emit `record.error` (level error); sync.record.conflict→emit `record.error` (level warn, message 'conflict'); sync.warning→emitter.warning (eventType `warning`); sync.entity-map.complete→stageComplete (eventType `stage.complete`) with counts {processed:recordsProcessed, succeeded:recordsCreated+recordsUpdated, failed:recordsErrored, skipped:recordsSkipped}; sync.push.response→emit `external.call.complete`. Everything else (run.start/config.loaded/connector.*/fetch.start/record.decision/record.saved/push.candidates/push.record + terminal run events) is INTENTIONALLY NOT mirrored. Run lifecycle (run.start/run.complete/run.fail) is owned by the caller, which calls progress.runStart()/complete()/fail() directly (IntegrationEngine.ts:375,398,404).

The discovery/pipeline event types (discovery.object.added, discovery.field.added, pk.classifier.invoked, pk.classifier.result, entity.generated, entity.skipped-no-pk) are emitted by IntegrationConnectorCreationPipeline.ts (lines 223,226,266,269,277,303,307,311) via emitter convenience methods, NOT by the sync engine. pk.classifier.result DataJSON carries {objectName, Confident, Nominee, Confidence, Strategy, Reason}.

COUNTS SHAPE: progress-artifacts/types.ts:111-117 — counts: {processed?, succeeded?, failed?, skipped?, totalKnown?} (lowercase in JSONL). The emitter ACCUMULATES counts into aggregateCounts on every emit (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:90-95). The reader re-aggregates from the whole stream (IntegrationProgressReader.ts:129-145). Over GQL these are exposed PascalCase: IntegrationRunCountsOutput {Processed, Succeeded, Failed, Skipped, TotalKnown} (IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:735-740, mapped at 4138-4148). totalKnown is in the type but NEVER set by any producer (always undefined) — a known gap.

DataJSON / ResumableStateJSON: On the polling query (IntegrationTailRunEvents) each event row carries DataJSON = JSON.stringify(ev.data) and ResumableStateJSON = JSON.stringify(ev.resumableState) (IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4102-4103). ResumableStateJSON is ONLY non-null on `checkpoint` events. On the subscription (IntegrationProgress), only DataJSON exists (no ResumableStateJSON field, IntegrationProgressResolver.ts:111) because checkpoint level is 'debug' and resumableState rides in the emit but the subscription notification omits it. Sync checkpoint resumableState shape (IntegrationEngine.ts:1348-1356): {watermark, afterKey, page, offset, cursor, batchIndex, recordsInMap}, emitted EVERY 25 batches (batchCount % 25 === 0).

WARNINGS: emitted via `warning` eventType. SyncWarning shape {code, stage, message, data} (types.ts:85-94). Reader aggregates all `warning` events into warnings[] + warningCount (Reader:146-159). Over GQL: WarningCount (number) and Warnings (string[] formatted EXACTLY as `[${code}] ${stage}: ${message}`, IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4134). The concrete warning CODES the sync engine emits: DEPENDENCY_LAYERING_DEGRADED (stage 'dependency-graph', IntegrationEngine.ts:779,800), SECOND_LAYER_EMPTY (stage=ExternalObjectName, line 885 — the canonical silent-empty signal, data={parentRecordCounts, parentsHadRows, category}), PUSH_SKIPPED (line 1813, data={operation,...}), ORPHANS_DETECTED (line 1992, data={orphanCount}), plus connector-forwarded warnings whose Code/Message/Data come from batch.Warnings (line 1238).

STAGE: For mirrored events the stage label is derived (SyncLogger.ts:222-225) from data.externalObjectName ?? entityMap ?? entity ?? objectName, defaulting to 'sync'. So a batch event's Stage == the ExternalObjectName.

LEVEL: 'info'|'warn'|'error'|'debug' (types.ts:77). record.error=error, warning=warn, checkpoint=debug, external.call.complete=debug (warn if status>=400).

GQL ENTRY POINTS: Polling — IntegrationListRuns / IntegrationGetRun / IntegrationTailRunEvents(runID, sinceSeq) reading <cwd>/logs/integration-runs/<runID>/{manifest.json,progress.jsonl,result.json}. Live push — single @Subscription IntegrationProgress(runID, companyIntegrationID, topic) on topic 'INTEGRATION_PROGRESS', payload IntegrationProgressNotification{RunID, CompanyIntegrationID, Kind, Topic, EventType, Seq, Message, Stage, Level, DataJSON}. The publish hook is wired in MJServer index.ts:646-658. Run terminal exitReason ∈ {completed, failed, killed, budget-exhausted, aborted} (types.ts:148). The run's runID IS the CompanyIntegrationRun.ID (IntegrationEngine.ts:368).

sourceRefs: packages/Integration/progress-artifacts/src/IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:78-116 (emit: seq, checkpoint latch, count accumulation, error/warning rollup, publish hook)
packages/MJServer/src/resolvers/IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:734-806 (GQL output types: IntegrationRunCountsOutput PascalCase, IntegrationRunEventOutput with DataJSON+ResumableStateJSON)
packages/MJServer/src/resolvers/IntegrationDiscoveryResolver.ts:4114-4148 (toRunSummaryArtifact Warnings format '[code] stage: message'; toCountsOutput lowercase→PascalCase)
knownGapsOrBugs: external.call.retry is declared in IntegrationProgressEventType (types.ts:60) and counted by the live-test harness (gql-live-harness.mjs:133) but has NO producer anywhere in engine or pipeline source — neither the sync engine nor the REST connector emits it. The adaptive rate limiter backs off on 429 (IntegrationEngine.ts:1210 reportRateOutcome) but emits no structured retry event. A live test asserting retryEvents>0 will always read zero. Gap, not a bug — needs a producer wired into the retry path.

counts.totalKnown / Counts.TotalKnown is defined in the counts shape (types.ts:116) and exposed over GQL but is NEVER set by any producer — always undefined. No event carries a denominator/total-expected. UI progress-bar percentage cannot be computed from the stream.

records.batch.complete events carry ONLY counts.processed (SyncLogger.ts:175-178, set from recordCount = fetched-record count). They do NOT carry succeeded/failed/skipped, and 'processed' here means FETCHED not APPLIED. The apply-side per-record outcomes (created/updated/skipped/errored) are NOT mirrored to the durable stream at all (sync.record.saved/decision are in the never-mirror default branch, SyncLogger.ts:211-215). Only the per-stage rollup (stage.complete) has the true applied quartet. A test must read stage.complete (not batch.complete) for accurate succeeded/failed/skipped.

The emitter's running aggregateCounts (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:90-95) double-counts processed: it sums BOTH records.batch.complete (fetched) AND stage.complete (applied) counts into the same aggregate, so the final aggregateCounts.processed in result.json over-reports. The Reader independently re-derives counts from the stream with the same flaw (Reader:129-145). Run.Counts.Processed is therefore not a clean records-applied total.

sync.connector.test, sync.push.record, sync.push.response, sync.run.cancelled are members of the SyncLogEvent union (SyncLogger.ts:42,54,55,60) but have NO emit() call site in IntegrationEngine.ts — dead vocabulary. (Cancellation is handled by breaking the fetch loop at IntegrationEngine.ts:1160-1163 and emitting nothing structured; the run still terminates via the normal complete/fail path.) sync.push.response IS referenced in forwardToEmitter (SyncLogger.ts:208) but since it is never emitted, external.call.complete is never produced by the sync path either.

Subscription notifications drop resumableState entirely — IntegrationProgressNotification has no ResumableStateJSON field (IntegrationProgressResolver.ts:71-112) and the publish hook only forwards event.data (index.ts:656). A live-resume client MUST use the polling IntegrationTailRunEvents query (which does serialize ResumableStateJSON, line 4103), not the subscription, to read checkpoint state.

The durable artifact root is <cwd>/logs/integration-runs (IntegrationProgressEmitter.ts:68); the reader defaults to the same (Reader:20). If MJAPI cwd differs between the run and the read, or the dir can't be created, createSyncProgressEmitter returns undefined (best-effort, IntegrationEngine.ts:372-376) and the run produces NO durable events at all — GQL queries return 'Run not found' even though the console (sync.*) stream still emitted. A live test must confirm the artifact dir is co-located with the reading process.
